epic_title: Conflict Coach v1 — AI-Mediated Conflict Resolution Web App
planning_assumptions: v1 targets P0 and P1 user stories only; P2 stories (US-16 through US-19) are deferred.
Convex Auth is used for authentication; Clerk is the swap-out plan if Convex Auth beta bugs block delivery — no data model change required.
All Convex functions are deployed to managed Convex cloud (not self-hosted) in v1.

Template lookup at case creation uses the current version for the selected category; if no template exists for that category, case creation returns INVALID_INPUT until an admin creates one or the seed script runs.

Solo mode is a view-level feature: the backend creates two partyStates rows for the same userId; private coaching message isolation requires functions to filter by caseId + userId + role to maintain per-party contexts when both parties share the same userId.
The privacy response filter uses token-substring matching (≥8 contiguous tokens) as specified in tech spec §6.3.2; embedding-similarity deferred to v1.1.
Synthesis generation is one-shot/non-streaming per tech spec TQ3; a 'Generating guidance...' loading state is shown.
Admin promotion to ADMIN role is a manual DB action in v1; no self-serve admin claim.
The CLOSED_ABANDONED auto-close after 30d inactivity uses a Convex daily cron (convex/crons.ts).
Message deletion and edit are not in v1; all messages are immutable per tech spec TQ6.
Typing indicators (presence) are deferred to v1.1 per tech spec §8.3.
The CLOSED_UNRESOLVED 24h cooling-off period is deferred to v1.1 per tech spec §5.2; unilateral close is immediate in v1.

All Convex query/mutation/action unit tests use the official convex-test npm package (in-memory Convex environment) rather than mocked-db Vitest, except pure utility modules with zero Convex ctx dependencies (errors.ts, privacyFilter.ts, compression.ts, prompts.ts).
tech_stack: Convex (database + queries + mutations + actions + realtime + file storage)
Claude Sonnet 4.5 (private coaching, joint coach, draft coach, synthesis)

milestones.label: Foundation — Schema, Utilities, Shared Components, CI
Core Flow — Auth, Case Creation, Invite, Private Coaching, Dashboard, Solo Mode
Joint Session — Synthesis, Joint Chat, Coach AI, Draft Coach, Case Closure
Admin & Polish — Templates, Audit Log, Landing Page, Accessibility

nfrs.name: Zero Cross-Party Private Content Leakage
nfrs.applies_to: All Convex queries/mutations/actions handling privateMessages or partyStates; all AI actions that assemble context; the privacy response filter; frontend components rendering private or AI-generated content
nfrs.ac_template: No Convex function returns another party's privateMessages or their detailed partyStates fields (beyond phase-level booleans: status and hasCompletedPC). The privacy response filter checks every AI output for ≥8 consecutive token matches from the other party's private transcript; on match, regenerates up to 2 times, then returns a generic fallback and flags for admin review. AI system prompts include the explicit no-quote / no-paraphrase instruction verbatim. The frontend never receives or renders the other party's raw private coaching transcript even transiently.

nfrs.name: Authentication & Authorization Enforcement
nfrs.applies_to: Every Convex query, mutation, and action; admin-gated routes; invite token generation and redemption
nfrs.ac_template: Every Convex function begins with ctx.auth.getUserIdentity() and throws UNAUTHENTICATED ConvexError if no session exists. Admin operations additionally verify user.role === 'ADMIN' server-side on every invocation; no client-provided role flag is trusted. Invite tokens are crypto-random 32 url-safe characters, single-use, and transition ACTIVE→CONSUMED atomically within one mutation. All admin actions (template create/publish/archive) write to the auditLog table with actorUserId and timestamp.

nfrs.name: AI Call Retry & Message Persistence
nfrs.applies_to: All Convex actions that call the Anthropic API: privateCoaching/generateAIResponse, synthesis/generate, jointChat/generateCoachResponse, draftCoach/generateResponse
nfrs.ac_template: User messages are inserted into the database (status=SENT) before the AI action is scheduled — message is never lost even if the action fails. AI actions retry once with 2s exponential backoff on 429 or network timeout (>30s). On final failure the message row transitions to ERROR status and a Retry button is rendered inline. On Anthropic content-filter rejection, a generic 'Coach can't respond to that' message is shown. Synthesis generation retries up to 2 times on privacy filter rejection; on third failure returns a generic fallback and writes a flag to auditLog.

nfrs.name: Latency & Throughput Targets
nfrs.applies_to: AI streaming actions (Private Coach, Coach, Draft Coach), joint chat message propagation, dashboard page load
nfrs.ac_template: AI streaming first token arrives within 2s for Private Coach and Draft Coach, within 3s for joint-chat Coach under normal Anthropic API load. Joint chat messages propagate from mutation insertion to both parties' reactive query updates in <500ms via Convex reactive channel. Dashboard first meaningful paint (case list visible) is <1s on a warm connection.

nfrs.name: WCAG 2.1 AA Accessibility
nfrs.applies_to: All user-facing React pages and components
nfrs.ac_template: All interactive elements are keyboard-navigable (Tab/Shift-Tab to focus, Enter/Space to activate). Chat regions use role='log' with aria-live='polite'; streaming messages announce 'Coach is replying' once, not on every character update. Modals trap focus on open and restore focus to trigger element on close. Draft Coach panel receives focus on its textarea when opened. All icons and non-decorative images have aria-label or alt text. Text color contrast meets WCAG 2.1 AA (≥4.5:1 for normal text). prefers-reduced-motion is respected: streaming cursor blink, route crossfades, and message fade-ins are disabled.

nfrs.name: Cross-Browser & Mobile Compatibility
nfrs.applies_to: All user-facing React pages and components
nfrs.ac_template: Feature works correctly in the latest 2 versions of Chrome, Safari, Firefox, and Edge on desktop. Mobile layouts function correctly on iOS 16+ Safari and Android 12+ Chrome. Draft Coach panel collapses to a bottom sheet on mobile (<768px breakpoint). No IE, Chrome <100, or Safari <15 compatibility shims needed.

Running `npx convex dev` in the cloned repo connects to this project without errors.

tasks.title: Convex schema definition (convex/schema.ts)
tasks.summary: Define all 10 Convex tables with exact field types, union literals, and indexes as specified in tech spec §3.1. This is the source of truth for the entire data model; all backend functions depend on this schema compiling without error. Includes: users, cases, partyStates, privateMessages, jointMessages, draftSessions, draftMessages, inviteTokens, templates, templateVersions, auditLog.
acceptance_criteria: `convex/schema.ts` matches the exact field definitions in tech spec §3.1 for all 10 tables.
All v.union() literals for status fields match the spec exactly (e.g. cases.status has all 7 literals including CLOSED_ABANDONED).

All indexes defined in the spec are present: cases.by_initiator, cases.by_invitee, partyStates.by_case, partyStates.by_case_and_user, privateMessages.by_case_and_user, privateMessages.by_case, jointMessages.by_case, draftSessions.by_case_and_user, draftMessages.by_draft_session, inviteTokens.by_token, inviteTokens.by_case, templates.by_category, templateVersions.by_template, auditLog.by_actor, users.by_email.
`npx convex dev` deploys the schema without type errors.
cases.schemaVersion is v.literal(1) as specified.
cases.isSolo (v.boolean()) is present for solo mode support.
Vitest unit test imports the schema and asserts at least one table and index definition (smoke test).

tasks.title: ConvexError error codes utility (convex/lib/errors.ts)
tasks.summary: Implement a typed ConvexError factory and the canonical error code set from tech spec §7.4: UNAUTHENTICATED, FORBIDDEN, NOT_FOUND, CONFLICT, INVALID_INPUT, TOKEN_INVALID, RATE_LIMITED, AI_ERROR, INTERNAL. Every Convex function throws these rather than bare Error objects, giving the client structured error handling.
acceptance_criteria: convex/lib/errors.ts exports a createError(code, message) helper that returns a ConvexError with { code, message } shape.
All 9 error codes from tech spec §7.4 are exported as string constants.
Vitest unit tests verify that each error code constant exists and that createError produces the expected ConvexError shape.
TypeScript compiles cleanly — no `any` types in the errors module.
The module is re-exported from a convex/lib/index.ts barrel for convenient import in other Convex files.

tasks.title: Auth and authorization helper functions (convex/lib/auth.ts)
tasks.summary: Implement the shared identity-check pattern used by every Convex function: requireAuth (returns user or throws UNAUTHENTICATED/USER_NOT_FOUND), requireParty (returns user and verifies they are the initiator or invitee of the given case or throws FORBIDDEN), and requireAdmin (checks user.role === 'ADMIN' or throws FORBIDDEN). Also includes getUserByEmail — the upsert-on-first-login helper.
acceptance_criteria: requireAuth(ctx) fetches ctx.auth.getUserIdentity(), looks up the user by email via getUserByEmail, and throws UNAUTHENTICATED or USER_NOT_FOUND (using T2 error codes) if either check fails.
requireParty(ctx, caseId) calls requireAuth and then verifies the user is the initiatorUserId or inviteeUserId of the case; throws FORBIDDEN otherwise.
requireAdmin(ctx) calls requireAuth and then verifies user.role === 'ADMIN'; throws FORBIDDEN otherwise.
getUserByEmail(ctx, email) upserts a users row on first login (role defaults to USER, createdAt set), returning the existing or newly created user.
convex-test unit tests for each helper cover: authenticated user passes, unauthenticated user throws UNAUTHENTICATED, non-party user throws FORBIDDEN, non-admin throws FORBIDDEN.
TypeScript compiles cleanly with full return type annotations.

tasks.title: Case state machine module (convex/lib/stateMachine.ts)
tasks.summary: Implement the case lifecycle state machine as a single helper module. Every mutation that transitions case status calls this module. Defines all valid transitions, validates preconditions, and returns the new status or throws CONFLICT if the transition is invalid. Prevents any client from skipping phases.
acceptance_criteria: Exports a transitionCase(currentStatus, targetStatus, preconditions) function that returns the new status or throws a CONFLICT ConvexError if the transition is disallowed.

Encodes all valid transitions from tech spec §5.1: DRAFT_PRIVATE_COACHING→BOTH_PRIVATE_COACHING (invitee accepts), BOTH_PRIVATE_COACHING→READY_FOR_JOINT (both PC complete + synthesis generated), READY_FOR_JOINT→JOINT_ACTIVE (first party enters), JOINT_ACTIVE→CLOSED_RESOLVED, JOINT_ACTIVE→CLOSED_UNRESOLVED, JOINT_ACTIVE→CLOSED_ABANDONED.
Rejects all other transition attempts with CONFLICT error.
convex-test unit tests cover: all valid transitions pass, all invalid transitions throw CONFLICT, attempting JOINT_ACTIVE→DRAFT_PRIVATE_COACHING throws CONFLICT.
TypeScript compiles cleanly with CaseStatus type derived from the schema union.
The module has zero side effects — it does not read or write the database directly.

tasks.title: Privacy response filter utility (convex/lib/privacyFilter.ts)
tasks.summary: Implement the server-side privacy enforcement layer that detects near-verbatim reproduction of one party's private content in AI outputs. Uses token-substring matching: tokenizes the other party's private messages and checks for any run of ≥8 consecutive tokens appearing in the candidate AI response. Used by synthesis generation and the Coach AI action before any output is committed or streamed to a user.
acceptance_criteria: Exports a checkPrivacyLeak(candidateText: string, otherPartyMessages: string[]) function returning { safe: boolean, matchedSnippet?: string }.
Tokenization is simple whitespace+punctuation split (deterministic, no external dependency).
Returns safe: false when any ≥8 consecutive token sequence from otherPartyMessages appears verbatim in candidateText.
Returns safe: true when no such sequence is found.

Vitest unit tests include: adversarial prompt with direct quote returns safe: false; paraphrase without direct quote returns safe: true; edge case of otherPartyMessages with < 8 tokens returns safe: true.
TypeScript compiles cleanly with no `any` types.
Function is pure — no database reads, no side effects.

tasks.title: Transcript compression and cost tracking utility (convex/lib/compression.ts)
tasks.summary: Implement the transcript compression strategy from tech spec §6.4: when total token count of messages would exceed a budget threshold (60k for user-facing generation, 10k for classification), the oldest 50% of messages are summarized by Claude Haiku and replaced with a single SUMMARY: message. Also includes the per-case cost tracking helpers: estimateCost(tokens, model) and checkCostCap(ctx, caseId) that reads the case's accumulated token counts and returns the cap status (ok | soft | hard).
acceptance_criteria: compressIfNeeded(messages, budget, anthropicClient) returns a (possibly compressed) messages array that fits within the token budget.
Compression calls claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 with the spec's compression prompt ('Summarize this conversation segment in 500 tokens or fewer...').
Content hash caching: if the same segment has been compressed before (same hash), the cached summary is returned without a new API call.
estimateCost(tokens, model) returns a dollar estimate using approximate Anthropic pricing for Sonnet and Haiku.
checkCostCap(ctx, caseId) returns 'ok' when accumulated cost < $2, 'soft' when $2 ≤ cost < $10, 'hard' when cost ≥ $10.
Vitest unit tests cover: compression is triggered when token count exceeds budget; idempotent on second call with same input; estimateCost returns a positive number for any positive token count.
The module imports the Anthropic SDK client from a passed-in dependency (not a module-level singleton) to remain testable.
tasks.decision: Inferred: the per-case cost tracking reads accumulated token counts from the partyStates and privateMessages/jointMessages tables. The spec mentions token counting in message rows; this utility queries those token fields to compute running totals. Not explicitly specified as a separate module but required by §6.6.

tasks.title: Prompt assembly module (convex/lib/prompts.ts)
tasks.summary: Implement the assemblePrompt() function from tech spec §6.3 that builds the system prompt and messages array for each AI role: PRIVATE_COACH, COACH, DRAFT_COACH, SYNTHESIS. Handles template injection for COACH and DRAFT_COACH roles, enforces the no-quote instruction in all prompts, and structures context correctly per role (private coach sees only one party's data; coach sees both synthesis texts but not raw private messages).
acceptance_criteria: assemblePrompt({ role, caseId, actingUserId, recentHistory, templateVersion? }) returns { system: string, messages: Message[] }.
PRIVATE_COACH role uses the hardcoded system prompt from tech spec §6.3.1 verbatim, with no template injection.
COACH role injects the template's globalGuidance and coachInstructions when a templateVersion is provided; uses baseline when not.
DRAFT_COACH role injects draftCoachInstructions; includes the drafting user's joint-chat history and their own synthesis but NOT the other party's synthesis.
SYNTHESIS role prompt includes the critical anti-quotation instruction verbatim ('You have access to both parties private content...' per spec §6.3.2).
All roles include the explicit no-quote anti-paraphrase rule in the system prompt.

Vitest unit tests verify: PRIVATE_COACH does not include other party data; COACH includes both synthesis texts; DRAFT_COACH excludes other party's synthesis; SYNTHESIS includes both parties' form data.
TypeScript compiles cleanly with full type annotations.

tasks.title: Shared ChatWindow React component (src/components/chat/)
tasks.summary: Build the reusable ChatWindow component used by Private Coaching, Joint Chat, and Draft Coach Panel. Includes MessageBubble (user/coach/system variants with streaming cursor), MessageInput (textarea, Enter-to-send, Shift-Enter newline), StreamingIndicator (blinking cursor), and the auto-scroll behavior (follows streaming unless user has scrolled up). Implements the visual language from the design doc: coach messages in --accent-subtle, user messages in --bg-surface.
acceptance_criteria: ChatWindow accepts props: messages[], onSend(text), isLoading, variant ('private' | 'joint' | 'draft'), and renders them correctly.

MessageBubble renders status=STREAMING with a blinking cursor (blinks at 500ms) and no copy button; status=COMPLETE with a copy button and timestamp; status=ERROR with error styling and a Retry button.
MessageInput sends on Enter, creates newline on Shift-Enter, disables Send while isLoading is true, but keeps the textarea enabled so users can pre-type.
Auto-scroll: sticks to latest message unless the user has scrolled up more than 50px; resumes on new message if the user scrolled back to bottom.
Chat region uses role='log' and aria-live='polite'. Streaming messages announce 'Coach is replying' once via an aria-live region, not on every character update.
prefers-reduced-motion: streaming cursor blink and message fade-in animation are disabled.
Vitest component tests: renders user and coach messages with correct visual variants; Send is disabled when isLoading; newline inserted on Shift-Enter.
Tailwind classes use CSS custom properties (--accent-subtle, --coach-accent, etc.) matching design doc §2.1 tokens.

tasks.title: Playwright test infrastructure and GitHub Actions CI
tasks.summary: Configure the full CI pipeline: GitHub Actions workflow with lint, typecheck, vitest unit tests, and Playwright E2E jobs. Set up Playwright with browser matrix (Chromium, Firefox, WebKit), fixture files (test users, case seeding helpers), and the CLAUDE_MOCK=true stub responder that returns canned AI responses with configurable streaming delays. The E2E job spins up a Convex dev deployment per run.
acceptance_criteria: .github/workflows/ci.yml defines four jobs in order: lint (ESLint + Prettier check), typecheck (tsc --noEmit), unit (vitest), e2e (Playwright).
Playwright is configured in playwright.config.ts with projects for chromium, firefox, and webkit.
A CLAUDE_MOCK=true environment variable activates a stub Anthropic client that returns deterministic canned responses with a configurable delay (default 100ms, simulating streaming).

Test fixtures include: createTestUser(email) helper, createTestCase(userId) helper, and a seedAdmin() helper that creates a user with role=ADMIN. vitest.config.ts integrates the convex-test environment (npm install convex-test added to dev dependencies) so Convex queries/mutations run against an in-memory Convex runtime in unit tests.

e2e/ directory is created with placeholder test files for each spec listed in tech spec §10.1 (auth.spec.ts, solo-full-flow.spec.ts, invite-flow.spec.ts, draft-coach.spec.ts, privacy.spec.ts, admin-templates.spec.ts, state-machine.spec.ts).
The CI pipeline runs successfully on a fresh checkout of the repo with no feature code yet (placeholder tests pass with 0 assertions).
README documents the cold-start local dev sequence from tech spec §11.3: git clone, npm install, copy .env.example, npx convex dev, npm run dev.

tasks.title: Convex Auth provider setup (magic link + Google OAuth)
tasks.summary: Configure Convex Auth in the backend with two providers: magic link (email-based one-time login link) and Google OAuth. Implements the identity model from tech spec §4.2: on first login, upsert a row in the users table via getUserByEmail. Configures the auth.config.ts / auth.ts Convex module to enable both providers.
acceptance_criteria: convex/auth.ts configures Convex Auth with the MagicLink email provider pointing to the configured email sender.
convex/auth.ts configures the Google OAuth provider using GOOGLE_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID and GOOGLE_OAUTH_CLIENT_SECRET env vars.
On first login via either provider, a row is upserted into the users table (email from identity, role=USER, createdAt=now).
ctx.auth.getUserIdentity() returns non-null for authenticated sessions in all Convex functions.
The Convex Auth module is exported from convex/auth.ts and imported in convex/http.ts for route registration.
A manual test confirms: magic-link email is sent and clicking it creates a session; Google OAuth redirect returns a valid session.
Session persists across browser reloads and expires after 30 days of inactivity (Convex Auth default behavior, no custom code needed).

tasks.title: Login, register, and logout frontend (src/routes/LoginPage.tsx + TopNav)

After submitting magic link, the form is replaced with 'Check your email for a sign-in link.' confirmation state.

Protected routes (all /cases/* and /dashboard) redirect unauthenticated users to /login.
No password field exists on the login page.
Page background uses --bg-canvas (#FAF8F5) per design doc §2.1.
Login card uses --bg-surface (#FFFFFF) with shadow-1 elevation per design doc §2.1.
Inter font is applied globally via CSS per design doc §2.2.
Primary 'Send magic link' button uses --accent (#6B8E7F) background per design doc §2.1.
Playwright auth.spec.ts: register new user via magic link (mock email capture), verify session persists on reload, log out and verify session is cleared.

tasks.title: cases/create mutation and invite token generation
tasks.summary: Implement the cases/create Convex mutation that creates a Case record, the initiator's partyStates row, and an invite token. Resolves the templateVersionId by looking up the current version for the chosen category. Generates a 32-character crypto-random url-safe invite token and inserts it into inviteTokens with status=ACTIVE. Returns caseId and the invite URL.
acceptance_criteria: cases/create accepts { category, mainTopic, description, desiredOutcome, templateId?, isSolo? } and is guarded by requireAuth.
Creates a cases row with status=DRAFT_PRIVATE_COACHING, schemaVersion=1, isSolo=false (or true if passed), timestamps, and the resolved templateVersionId.
Resolves templateVersionId: queries templates.by_category for the given category, reads currentVersionId; throws INVALID_INPUT if no template exists for the category.
Creates the initiator's partyStates row with role=INITIATOR and the form fields set.
Generates invite token: crypto.randomUUID().replace(/-/g,'') + crypto.randomUUID().replace(/-/g,'') trimmed to 32 chars; inserts into inviteTokens with status=ACTIVE.
Returns { caseId, inviteUrl } where inviteUrl = SITE_URL + '/invite/' + token.
requireAuth is called first; unauthenticated callers receive UNAUTHENTICATED error.
convex-test unit tests: token is exactly 32 url-safe chars; INVALID_INPUT thrown when no template for category.
tasks.decision: Inferred: the mutation resolves the templateVersionId from the category at case-creation time per spec §3.2 invariant 1. This requires at least one template to exist in the DB — satisfied by T22 (seed) in dev and by admin template creation in production.

tasks.title: invites/redeem and cases/updateMyForm mutations
tasks.summary: Implement two mutations: invites/redeem binds the current user as the case invitee, creates their partyStates row, and marks the token CONSUMED atomically; cases/updateMyForm updates the caller's partyStates form fields (mainTopic, description, desiredOutcome). Both are used by the invite acceptance flow after the invitee logs in.
acceptance_criteria: invites/redeem({ token }) is guarded by requireAuth, queries inviteTokens.by_token, throws TOKEN_INVALID if status ≠ ACTIVE.
In a single transaction: sets cases.inviteeUserId to caller's userId, creates invitee partyStates row (role=INVITEE), marks inviteTokens.status=CONSUMED with consumedAt and consumedByUserId.
Reusing a consumed token (second call with same token) returns TOKEN_INVALID.
cases/updateMyForm({ caseId, mainTopic, description, desiredOutcome }) is guarded by requireParty, updates caller's partyStates row with the provided fields and sets formCompletedAt=now.
cases/updateMyForm only ever updates the partyStates row where userId == callerUserId — it never touches the other party's partyStates row, verified by requireParty filtering on callerUserId.
Both mutations validate all required fields are non-empty strings; throw INVALID_INPUT if not.

convex-test unit tests: token consumption is atomic and idempotent on retry; second redemption throws TOKEN_INVALID; non-party caller to updateMyForm throws FORBIDDEN; updateMyForm called by party A cannot modify party B's partyStates row.

tasks.title: Case creation form and invite share screen frontend
tasks.summary: Build the /cases/new multi-step form (category radio cards, main topic with 140-char soft limit, description textarea with privacy lock icon, desired outcome textarea, other party name) and the post-create invite share screen (/cases/:id/invite) with a copyable invite link, three preset share options, and the option to start private coaching immediately. Also includes the CaseDetail routing shell that redirects to the correct phase subview based on case status.
acceptance_criteria: /cases/new renders 5 sequential form steps as described in design doc §4.5: category radio cards (5 options), main topic with 140-char counter, description with privacy lock + tooltip ('Only you and the AI coach will see this'), desired outcome, other party name.
Solo mode 'New Solo Case' checkbox is hidden under an Advanced disclosure, not visible by default.
Submit calls cases/create mutation; on success routes to /cases/:id/invite.

/cases/:id/invite shows the invite URL in a large copyable field with a 'Copy link' primary button, three share options (email/text/copy), and the 'What should I tell them?' expandable section with suggested language.
'Start your private coaching now →' link on the post-create screen routes to /cases/:id/private.
CaseDetail (/cases/:caseId) reads case status and redirects: DRAFT_PRIVATE_COACHING or BOTH_PRIVATE_COACHING → /private; READY_FOR_JOINT → /ready; JOINT_ACTIVE → /joint; CLOSED_* → /closed.
Form validates all required fields before submit; shows inline errors.
Form inputs use --border-strong for border styling per design doc §2.1.
Input hover states use --bg-surface-subtle per design doc §2.1.
Secondary/helper text labels use --text-secondary for lower-prominence content per design doc §2.1.
Playwright invite-flow.spec.ts: User A completes the form, invite link is generated and displayed.

tasks.title: Invite acceptance page frontend (src/routes/InviteAcceptPage.tsx)
tasks.summary: Build /invite/:token page with two states: logged-out state (explains product, prompts sign-in, stashes token in URL/localStorage through auth flow) and logged-in/unredeemed state (shows initiator's mainTopic + category with privacy label and an Accept button). Accepting calls invites/redeem then routes to the invitee's case form via cases/updateMyForm flow.
acceptance_criteria: Logged-out state renders: 'Alex has invited you to work through something together.', product description, 'Sign in to continue' button; token is preserved through the auth redirect.

Logged-in/unredeemed state renders: initiator's mainTopic (via cases/get from T16) and category, with privacy label 'Alex wrote this in the shared summary. You'll have your own private space to share your perspective.', and an Accept button only (no Decline).
Accept calls invites/redeem; if the token is already CONSUMED, renders a TOKEN_INVALID error with 'Go to Dashboard' option.

A non-party, non-admin user who navigates to /invite/:token where the token is CONSUMED receives the TOKEN_INVALID error and cannot access any case data (server-side FORBIDDEN on cases/get; client shows TOKEN_INVALID).
After accepting, user is shown the invitee's case form (same multi-step form as T14 but for their partyStates fields) before being routed to /cases/:id/private.
Token persists through a full OAuth round-trip: user clicks Accept, is redirected to /login for Google OAuth, returns to /invite/:token and can accept without re-entering the URL.
Playwright invite-flow.spec.ts: User B opens invite link in a second browser context, registers, accepts invitation, both users see the case in their dashboards.
tasks.decision: A 'Decline' affordance was considered to let the invitee explicitly reject the case at the invite page. The PRD §3.4 and tech spec §4.4 do not define a decline action — the invite flow ends at 'Accept' (token consumed, partyStates row created). Additionally, the only valid CLOSED_ABANDONED transition in the T4 state machine is from JOINT_ACTIVE (30d inactivity cron); transitioning from DRAFT_PRIVATE_COACHING to CLOSED_ABANDONED would be rejected at runtime. Rather than invent a new state transition or introduce a soft-delete path not in the spec, the simpler and PRD-faithful choice is to omit the Decline button entirely. If the invitee chooses not to participate, the case remains in DRAFT_PRIVATE_COACHING and the invitee simply does not engage; the token is already CONSUMED, so the slot cannot be taken by another user.

tasks.title: Dashboard, case detail, and private coaching Convex queries
tasks.summary: Implement the reactive Convex queries powering the dashboard, case detail views, and private coaching: cases/list (current user's cases), cases/get (single case by id, party-gated), cases/partyStates (returns caller's full PartyState plus a restricted { status, hasCompletedPC } view of the other party — never the other party's form fields or private content), and privateCoaching/myMessages (returns only the calling user's private messages for a case, enforcing strict per-user isolation).
acceptance_criteria: cases/list({}) is guarded by requireAuth and returns all cases where the caller is initiatorUserId or inviteeUserId, ordered by updatedAt desc.
cases/get({ caseId }) is guarded by requireParty and returns the full Case row.

cases/partyStates({ caseId }) is guarded by requireParty and returns { self: PartyState, otherPhaseOnly: { privateCoachingCompletedAt: boolean, formCompletedAt: boolean } } — otherPhaseOnly contains only two booleans, never form field text.

privateCoaching/myMessages({ caseId }) is guarded by requireParty and returns only PrivateMessages where userId == callerUserId, ordered by createdAt; it never returns the other party's messages even if called with a valid caseId by an authenticated party.

convex-test unit tests: cases/list returns only the caller's cases; cases/partyStates for party A never includes party B's mainTopic, description, or desiredOutcome fields; privateCoaching/myMessages called by party A returns only party A's messages and never party B's messages.
TypeScript compiles cleanly; the otherPhaseOnly return type is a narrow interface with only the two boolean fields.
All queries are reactive (use ctx.db.query, not ctx.db.get in a loop) so the Convex client subscribes and receives push updates.

tasks.title: Dashboard frontend (src/routes/Dashboard.tsx)
tasks.summary: Build /dashboard showing the user's cases grouped into Active and Closed sections. Each case row shows the other party's name, category, created date, current status indicator glyph (●/○/◐/◼), last activity time, and an Enter button. Implements the design doc §4.4 layout including the empty state and skeleton loading screens.
acceptance_criteria: Dashboard subscribes to cases/list via useQuery and renders Active and Closed case sections.

Each case row shows: other party's display name (or 'Pending' if inviteeUserId is null), category, created date, status glyph (● green = your turn, ○ gray = waiting, ◐ amber = ready for joint, ◼ neutral = closed), last activity time, and Enter button.

Status glyph logic: ● when the current user has a pending action (e.g. joint chat active and no recent message from them); ○ when waiting for the other party; ◐ when status is READY_FOR_JOINT; ◼ when CLOSED_*.
Active section shows all non-CLOSED_* cases; Closed section is collapsed by default and shows CLOSED_* cases.
Empty state renders: 'No cases yet. When you're ready to work through something, start a new case.'
Loading state: skeleton of 3 case rows (using shadcn/ui Skeleton) shown while useQuery is loading.
+ New Case primary button in top right routes to /cases/new.
Dashboard first meaningful paint (case list visible) is <1s on warm connection.
Page background uses --bg-canvas (#FAF8F5) per design doc §2.1.
Case row cards use --bg-surface (#FFFFFF) with shadow-1 elevation per design doc §2.1.
Metadata text (created date, last activity time) uses --text-secondary per design doc §2.1.
Primary action button uses --accent (#6B8E7F) per design doc §2.1.
Playwright invite-flow.spec.ts: both users see the case in their dashboards after invite acceptance.

tasks.title: Private coaching Convex mutations (sendUserMessage, markComplete)
tasks.summary: Implement the two Convex mutations for private coaching: privateCoaching/sendUserMessage (persists the user's message row with status=SENT and schedules the generateAIResponse action in T42) and privateCoaching/markComplete (sets privateCoachingCompletedAt on the caller's partyStates row; when both parties have set this field, schedules synthesis/generate action in T23). Strict userId isolation: every DB access in these mutations filters by the calling userId. The AI generation action is in T42; the myMessages reactive query is in T16.
acceptance_criteria: privateCoaching/sendUserMessage({ caseId, content }) is guarded by requireParty, inserts a privateMessages row (role=USER, status=SENT) for callerUserId, then schedules privateCoaching/generateAIResponse action (T42).

privateCoaching/markComplete({ caseId }) is guarded by requireParty; sets caller's partyStates.privateCoachingCompletedAt=now; when both parties have set this field, schedules synthesis/generate action (T23).
All DB writes in sendUserMessage and markComplete filter by callerUserId — no mutation touches the other party's rows.
markComplete is idempotent: calling it twice does not throw and does not create duplicate synthesis jobs.
Both mutations use requireParty (T3) for access control; unauthenticated callers receive UNAUTHENTICATED; non-party callers receive FORBIDDEN.

convex-test unit tests: sendUserMessage inserts a privateMessages row with correct role=USER and status=SENT for callerUserId only; markComplete called once schedules synthesis when both parties are complete; markComplete called twice is idempotent (no duplicate scheduler entries).
Playwright state-machine.spec.ts: markComplete is idempotent — calling it twice does not throw and does not create duplicate synthesis jobs.

tasks.title: Private coaching frontend (src/routes/PrivateCoachingView.tsx)
tasks.summary: Build /cases/:caseId/private using the shared ChatWindow (T8). Includes the persistent privacy banner with lock icon and 'Learn more' modal, the streaming AI message rendering, the 'Mark private coaching complete' footer CTA with a confirmation dialog showing message count, and real-time status updates via Convex reactive queries.
acceptance_criteria: PrivateCoachingView subscribes to privateCoaching/myMessages({ caseId }) (implemented in T16) and renders messages in ChatWindow.
Persistent PrivacyBanner at top: '🔒 This conversation is private to you. Jordan will never see any of it. [Learn more]'. Clicking 'Learn more' opens a Dialog explaining the privacy model.
Coach messages rendered in --accent-subtle bubbles, left-aligned, with Sparkles icon. User messages in --bg-surface, right-aligned.

Clicking the CTA opens a confirmation Dialog: 'You've had {N} messages with the Coach. Ready to move on to the joint session with {other party name}?' with 'Continue Coaching' and 'Mark Complete' buttons.

The component never fetches or renders the other party's private messages — only subscribes to privateCoaching/myMessages.
Playwright solo-full-flow.spec.ts: initiator completes private coaching; invitee completes private coaching; both show the waiting state before synthesis is generated.

tasks.title: Solo mode backend (isSolo case creation variant)
tasks.summary: Extend cases/create to handle isSolo=true: when true, both the INITIATOR and INVITEE partyStates rows are created immediately for the same userId, the inviteTokens row is skipped (no token needed), and cases.inviteeUserId is set to the same userId as initiatorUserId. Solo cases are flagged in the DB and any admin view.
acceptance_criteria: cases/create with isSolo=true creates two partyStates rows for the same userId: one with role=INITIATOR, one with role=INVITEE.
cases.inviteeUserId is set equal to cases.initiatorUserId for solo cases.
No inviteTokens row is created for solo cases.
cases.isSolo=true is persisted and returned by cases/list and cases/get queries.
Solo cases are visually flagged in the dashboard (T17 must show a distinct badge/label for isSolo=true cases).

Solo case privacy isolation: private coaching message functions filter by caseId + userId + role (not just caseId + userId) so that INITIATOR-role messages and INVITEE-role messages remain separate even when both partyStates rows share the same userId.
convex-test unit test: for a solo case, both partyStates rows exist for the same userId with different roles; querying privateMessages as INITIATOR does not return INVITEE messages.
Solo cases appear in audit log entries with an isSolo flag in metadata to prevent confusion with real user data.
tasks.decision: Inferred: solo mode's two partyStates rows share the same userId. To maintain separate private coaching contexts (required by US-13 AC), private coaching functions must use caseId + userId + role as the message partition key, not caseId + userId alone. This is not explicitly stated in the schema section but is logically required by the isolation guarantee.

tasks.title: Solo mode frontend (PartyToggle, useSoloActingParty hook)
tasks.summary: Build the solo mode UI: PartyToggle segmented control in TopNav (colored --coach-accent), useSoloActingParty hook that reads ?as=initiator|invitee query param and returns the corresponding partyStates userId and role, and integration with all case views so every query and mutation uses the toggle-selected party context. A 'New Solo Case' option on the dashboard routes to the creation form with solo mode pre-selected.
acceptance_criteria: PartyToggle renders in TopNav only when isSolo=true for the current case, showing 'Viewing as: [Alex] [Jordan]' as a segmented control with --coach-accent color.

All Convex query subscriptions in case views (PrivateCoachingView, ReadyForJointView, JointChatView) pass both the toggled userId and role so each view correctly simulates the selected party's private context — no query subscription accidentally crosses party boundaries when the toggle changes.
A prominent 'Solo Test Case' banner (--coach-accent background) appears on all solo case views to prevent confusion.
'+ New Solo Case' option on Dashboard opens /cases/new with the Advanced disclosure expanded and solo checkbox pre-checked.
Playwright solo-full-flow.spec.ts: toggling from Initiator to Invitee in the same browser session switches the private coaching context and shows different messages.

tasks.title: Seed data script (convex/seed.ts)
tasks.summary: Implement the admin-callable dev seed script from tech spec §11.4 that creates one admin user and three default templates (workplace, family, personal) each with a minimal globalGuidance and an initial templateVersion. Required for local dev and CI E2E tests to have valid templates for case creation.
acceptance_criteria: convex/seed.ts exports a runSeed internal action that: creates a user with email='admin@conflictcoach.app' and role=ADMIN, creates three templates (category: workplace, family, personal), each with a templateVersion containing a brief globalGuidance string.
The seed is idempotent: running it twice does not create duplicate users or templates (checks by email / category before inserting).
A `npx convex run seed:runSeed` command succeeds in local dev and creates the expected records.
The CI E2E Playwright job runs seed before the test suite so all tests start with valid template data.
The seed script is guarded to only run in dev/test deployments (throws an error in production via an environment check).
convex-test unit test: running runSeed twice results in exactly 1 admin user and 3 templates (idempotency verified).

tasks.title: Synthesis Convex action (synthesis/generate)
tasks.summary: Implement the synthesis/generate Convex action triggered by both parties marking private coaching complete (scheduled by T18's markComplete mutation). Calls Claude Sonnet with both parties' private content, validates the strict JSON output { forInitiator, forInvitee }, runs each text through the privacy filter (T5), retries up to 2 times on filter failure, writes results to partyStates.synthesisText, and advances the case to READY_FOR_JOINT. The mySynthesis reactive query is implemented separately in T43.
acceptance_criteria: synthesis/generate({ caseId }) is an action (not mutation) guarded by an internal-only caller check; reads both parties' privateMessages and partyStates form fields.
Calls assemblePrompt with role=SYNTHESIS (T7), calls Claude Sonnet (non-streaming), validates JSON output matches { forInitiator: string, forInvitee: string } schema; throws AI_ERROR if invalid.
Runs checkPrivacyLeak(forInitiator, partyBMessages) and checkPrivacyLeak(forInvitee, partyAMessages); retries generation up to 2 more times on leak detection.
On third failure, writes a generic fallback synthesis text and writes a flag to auditLog (action=SYNTHESIS_FALLBACK_USED).
Writes synthesisText and synthesisGeneratedAt to each party's partyStates row, then calls transitionCase to READY_FOR_JOINT within a single mutation.

convex-test unit tests: privacy filter rejection triggers retry; generic fallback text is written and auditLog entry created after 2 retries; synthesisText is written to both parties' partyStates rows.
Playwright solo-full-flow.spec.ts: after both parties mark PC complete, synthesis text appears for each party in the ReadyForJoint view.

tasks.title: Ready-for-Joint view frontend (src/routes/ReadyForJointView.tsx)
tasks.summary: Build /cases/:caseId/ready showing the per-party synthesis in a private-tinted card with a lock icon and 'Enter Joint Session →' primary CTA. Once the user enters, the case transitions to JOINT_ACTIVE and they are routed to the joint chat. The synthesis remains accessible from a 'View my guidance' link in the joint chat nav.
acceptance_criteria: ReadyForJointView subscribes to jointChat/mySynthesis and renders the synthesis text in a card with --private-tint background and a lock icon.
Card header reads: '🔒 Private to you — [other party] has their own version'.
Synthesis text is rendered as markdown (headings, bold) using a markdown renderer.
'Enter Joint Session →' is a large primary button; clicking it calls a mutation to transition the case to JOINT_ACTIVE and routes to /cases/:caseId/joint.
A 'Take your time reading this. When you're ready:' paragraph precedes the CTA.
'(Other party will see you've entered when they enter too.)' caption below the CTA.
The view shows a loading skeleton while mySynthesis is loading.
Playwright solo-full-flow.spec.ts: synthesis text is visible; entering joint session routes to joint chat.

tasks.title: Joint chat Convex mutations (sendUserMessage, enterJointChat)
tasks.summary: Implement two joint chat mutations: jointChat/sendUserMessage (inserts joint message with authorUserId, schedules Coach evaluation) and jointChat/enterJointChat (handles the READY_FOR_JOINT→JOINT_ACTIVE transition when the first party enters). The reactive messages query is implemented separately in T37.
acceptance_criteria: jointChat/sendUserMessage({ caseId, content }) is guarded by requireParty + case must be in JOINT_ACTIVE status (throws CONFLICT otherwise).
Inserts JointMessage row with authorType=USER, authorUserId=caller, status=SENT, then schedules jointChat/generateCoachResponse action with triggerType='USER_MESSAGE'.
jointChat/enterJointChat({ caseId }) mutation transitions READY_FOR_JOINT→JOINT_ACTIVE (via T4 state machine) when called; subsequent calls by the second party are idempotent.
Attempting sendUserMessage when case is not JOINT_ACTIVE returns CONFLICT error.
convex-test unit tests: sendUserMessage in DRAFT_PRIVATE_COACHING state throws CONFLICT; enterJointChat is idempotent on second call.
Playwright state-machine.spec.ts: attempt to send joint message when case is in private coaching phase — must fail with CONFLICT.

tasks.title: Joint chat frontend (src/routes/JointChatView.tsx) including closure modal and confirmation banner
tasks.summary: Build /cases/:caseId/joint as the primary shared conversation view. Uses ChatWindow (T8) with joint-chat configuration. Shows messages from both parties and the Coach with distinct avatar colors (initiator=soft blue, invitee=soft rose, Coach=lavender). Coach intervention messages have a --coach-accent left border and ⟡ glyph. Includes real-time reactive subscription to jointChat/messages (T37). Also owns the inline closure Dialog (three closure options triggered by 'Close' in top nav) and the confirmation banner shown when the other party has proposed closure — these are inline components within JointChatView and do not require a separate ticket.
acceptance_criteria: JointChatView subscribes to jointChat/messages({ caseId }) (implemented in T37) and renders all messages in the ChatWindow.
Message authorship visualization: initiator avatar = --party-initiator color, invitee avatar = --party-invitee color, Coach messages = --coach-accent left border + ⟡ glyph.
Timestamps appear on hover for each message.
Input bar shows: [Type your message...] textarea, [✨ Draft with Coach] button, [Send] button. Users can type and Send directly without using Draft Coach.
Clicking '✨ Draft with Coach' opens the DraftCoachPanel (T29).
'My guidance' link in top nav opens a sheet/modal with the user's synthesis text (calls jointChat/mySynthesis).

Clicking 'Close' in joint chat top nav opens an inline Dialog with three options: Resolved (with summary textarea, required), Not Resolved (with optional message textarea, danger styling), Take a Break (closes dialog, stays in JOINT_ACTIVE). Resolved calls jointChat/proposeClosure (T30); Not Resolved calls jointChat/unilateralClose (T30).

When the other party has proposed closure, a confirmation banner appears above the chat input: '📬 [Party] has proposed resolving this case. Their summary: [...]' with [Confirm] and [Reject and keep talking] buttons. Confirm calls jointChat/confirmClosure (T30) and routes to /cases/:id/closed.
A 'Coach is thinking...' inline indicator appears while generateCoachResponse action is in progress.
Space is reserved below input for 'Jordan is typing...' presence indicator (deferred to v1.1 — renders empty div in v1).

JointChatView never subscribes to or renders the other party's private coaching messages or raw partyStates form fields; the 'My guidance' link invokes only jointChat/mySynthesis (T43), which returns only the calling party's synthesisText.
In solo mode, the PartyToggle (useSoloActingParty from T21) is active and all query subscriptions pass the toggled userId and role.
Playwright solo-full-flow.spec.ts: both parties exchange messages; Coach response appears; guidance link shows synthesis; propose closure modal opens; confirmation banner appears for other party.
tasks.decision: Inferring CSS custom property names --party-initiator and --party-invitee for the initiator/invitee avatar colors. Design doc §4.9 describes these as 'soft blue' and 'soft rose' respectively but defines no CSS custom property names. Naming them as custom properties for consistency with the rest of the design token system; suggested values --party-initiator: #DBEAFE (Tailwind blue-100) and --party-invitee: #FFE4E6 (Tailwind rose-100), subject to design review against WCAG contrast on --bg-surface. Additionally, the closure modal Dialog and confirmation banner are implemented as inline components within JointChatView rather than in a separate ticket (T31). This eliminates the circular dependency that would arise if T31 owned these components while also depending on T26 (JointChatView would need to import the modal from T31, but T31 depends on T26 to exist). Moving these small inline components into T26 is structurally cleaner: they are rendered inside JointChatView's JSX and do not need a separate module. T31 is then free to own only ClosedCaseView, which stands alone independently of JointChatView.

tasks.title: Coach AI action (generateCoachResponse: Haiku gate, facilitation, @-mention, timer)
tasks.summary: Implement jointChat/generateCoachResponse action with the Haiku classification gate from tech spec §6.3.3. First calls Haiku to classify the triggering message as INFLAMMATORY, PROGRESS, QUESTION_TO_COACH, or NORMAL_EXCHANGE. Only generates a Coach response for non-NORMAL_EXCHANGE, on @-mention, or on the 5-exchange timer. Coach response is assembled via assemblePrompt(COACH) using both parties' synthesis texts (not raw private messages), run through the privacy filter, and streamed into a JointMessage row.
acceptance_criteria: generateCoachResponse({ caseId, triggerType }) calls claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 first to classify the last user message as INFLAMMATORY | PROGRESS | QUESTION_TO_COACH | NORMAL_EXCHANGE.
Coach generates a response for: INFLAMMATORY (always), PROGRESS (always), QUESTION_TO_COACH (always), NORMAL_EXCHANGE only when triggerType='TIMER' (5+ exchanges with no Coach input).
Coach context uses both parties' synthesisText (from partyStates) — NOT their raw privateMessages — assembled via assemblePrompt(COACH).
Response is run through checkPrivacyLeak against both parties' private message texts; retried up to 2 times on leak; falls back to generic response on third failure.
Streams Coach response into a JointMessage row (authorType=COACH, isIntervention=true for INFLAMMATORY responses).
Cost cap check: if hard cap hit, Coach posts a cost-limit message and does not call Sonnet.
AI retry on 429/timeout with 2s backoff; sets message status=ERROR on final failure.
Vitest unit tests: NORMAL_EXCHANGE without TIMER trigger returns without generating Coach message; INFLAMMATORY triggers Coach response; privacy filter rejection triggers retry.
Playwright solo-full-flow.spec.ts: @-mentioning Coach produces a Coach response; inflammatory message produces a Coach intervention.

tasks.title: Draft Coach generateResponse Convex action
tasks.summary: Implement the draftCoach/generateResponse Convex action that drives the AI-assisted draft conversation. Assembles context via assemblePrompt(DRAFT_COACH), detects readiness signals in user messages, produces a structured finalDraft JSON output when the user is ready, and streams the AI reply into the draft session. CRUD mutations (startSession, sendMessage, sendFinalDraft, discardSession) are in T38; the session reactive query is in T39.
acceptance_criteria: draftCoach/generateResponse({ sessionId }) assembles context via assemblePrompt(DRAFT_COACH) — includes caller's joint-chat history and their own synthesis, NOT the other party's synthesis or private messages.

Readiness detection: if the user message matches any of the readiness signals from tech spec §6.3.4, or if the user clicks 'Draft it for me' (posts a canonical trigger message), generateResponse produces a JSON response { draft: '...' } and sets draftSessions.finalDraft.
Privacy: generateResponse context query never fetches otherPartyMessages or otherParty synthesisText.
AI retry on 429/timeout with 2s backoff; sets message status=ERROR on final failure.
Cost cap check: if hard cap hit, returns a cost-limit message and does not call Sonnet.
Draft Coach generateResponse streams first token within 2s under normal Anthropic API load (verified in E2E via Playwright timing assertion with CLAUDE_MOCK=true delivering first token within 100ms).
Vitest unit tests: privacy context assembly does not include other party data; readiness signal detection triggers finalDraft production.

tasks.title: Draft Coach panel frontend (DraftCoachPanel + DraftReadyCard)
tasks.summary: Build the Draft Coach side panel that slides in from the right (420px wide, full height on desktop; full-screen bottom sheet on mobile). Includes the privacy header with lock icon, the ChatWindow instance for the draft conversation, the 'Draft it for me' button, and when finalDraft is populated the DraftReadyCard with four action buttons: Send this message, Edit before sending, Keep refining, Discard.

Mobile (<768px): panel renders as a shadcn/ui Sheet from the bottom, not a side panel.
On open, focus moves to the Draft Coach panel textarea.
Playwright draft-coach.spec.ts: iterate with Draft Coach; verify 'Generate Draft' does NOT auto-send; verify only 'Send' posts to joint chat; verify Discard sends nothing.

tasks.title: Case closure Convex mutations (proposeClosure, confirmClosure, unilateralClose)
tasks.summary: Implement the three closure mutations: jointChat/proposeClosure (sets closureProposed on partyStates, stores summary on case), jointChat/confirmClosure (confirms if other party has proposed, transitions to CLOSED_RESOLVED), and jointChat/unilateralClose (transitions to CLOSED_UNRESOLVED, records reason). The CLOSED_ABANDONED daily cron is implemented separately in T40.

jointChat/confirmClosure({ caseId }) checks the other party's partyStates.closureProposed=true; if true, transitions case to CLOSED_RESOLVED via T4 state machine and sets cases.closedAt.

All closure mutations are guarded by requireParty + case must be JOINT_ACTIVE.
Attempting confirmClosure when no proposal exists throws CONFLICT.

acceptance_criteria: ClosedCaseView at /cases/:caseId/closed shows: case header (other party name, category, closure date, outcome label — RESOLVED / UNRESOLVED), closure summary if CLOSED_RESOLVED.
Three nav tabs: Joint Chat (full joint transcript, read-only), My Private Coaching (caller's private messages from T16), My Guidance (caller's synthesisText from T43).
