tasks.title: Admin template Convex mutations (create, publishNewVersion, archive) and audit log writes
tasks.summary: Implement the three admin template write operations from tech spec §7.2: admin/templates/create mutation, admin/templates/publishNewVersion mutation (creates new templateVersion row, updates currentVersionId), and admin/templates/archive mutation (sets archivedAt). All three write to the auditLog table. Read-only admin queries (listAll, templateVersions/list, templates/list, auditLog/list) are implemented separately in T41.
acceptance_criteria: admin/templates/create({ category, name, globalGuidance, coachInstructions?, draftCoachInstructions? }) is guarded by requireAdmin; creates templates row and initial templateVersions row with version=1; writes TEMPLATE_CREATED to auditLog.

admin/templates/publishNewVersion({ templateId, globalGuidance, ... }) is guarded by requireAdmin; inserts new templateVersions row (version incremented), updates templates.currentVersionId; writes TEMPLATE_PUBLISHED to auditLog; existing cases are unaffected (their templateVersionId is immutable).
admin/templates/archive({ templateId }) is guarded by requireAdmin; sets templates.archivedAt=now; archived templates disappear from templates/list but are still resolvable by pinned cases.
convex-test unit tests: publishNewVersion does not mutate the previous templateVersion row; archive hides from templates/list; admin mutations by a non-admin user throw FORBIDDEN.
Playwright admin-templates.spec.ts: create template, publish v2, verify pinned case still uses v1, archive template.

tasks.title: Admin templates frontend (TemplatesListPage + TemplateEditPage)
tasks.summary: Build /admin/templates (table of templates with Category, Name, Current Version, Status, Pinned Cases Count columns and + New Template button) and /admin/templates/:id (two-pane layout: edit form on left, version history timeline on right; Publish New Version primary button; Archive danger button with confirmation modal).
acceptance_criteria: /admin/templates is only accessible to users with role=ADMIN: a client-side route guard redirects non-admin users to /dashboard on navigation, and all underlying Convex queries (admin/templates/listAll, admin/templateVersions/list from T41) return FORBIDDEN when called by a non-admin user (server-side requireAdmin guard on every invocation).
TemplatesListPage shows a table with columns: Category | Name | Current Version | Status (Active/Archived) | Pinned Cases Count. Clicking a row navigates to the edit view.

TemplateEditPage two-pane layout: left pane has form fields (category select, name, globalGuidance markdown textarea, coachInstructions textarea, draftCoachInstructions textarea, notes); right pane shows version history timeline with each version's date, admin name, notes, and 'View' button (read-only diff).
'Publish New Version' button calls admin/templates/publishNewVersion; success toast confirms publish.
'Archive Template' button shows a confirmation Dialog with a count of pinned cases that will continue using the old version; calls admin/templates/archive on confirm.
Archived templates show a disabled/grayed 'Archived' badge and do not appear in the category picker on case creation.
Playwright admin-templates.spec.ts: admin creates template, publishes v2, verifies v2 is current, archives, verifies it's hidden from category picker.

tasks.title: Audit log frontend (src/routes/admin/AuditLogPage.tsx)
tasks.summary: Build /admin/audit showing a filterable, read-only table of all auditLog entries. Columns: Actor | Action | Target | Timestamp. Clicking a row opens a Drawer with the full JSON metadata payload. Not editable.
acceptance_criteria: /admin/audit is gated by requireAdmin (client-side redirect for non-admins).
Table shows all auditLog rows from the admin/auditLog/list Convex query (implemented in T41, guarded by requireAdmin), ordered by createdAt desc.
Columns: Actor (user email), Action (e.g. TEMPLATE_PUBLISHED), Target (targetType + targetId), Timestamp.
Clicking a row opens a shadcn/ui Drawer showing the full metadata JSON, formatted for readability.
Filter input (text search on Action or Actor) is client-side and does not require a new query.
The table is not editable — no edit/delete controls exist.
Skeleton loading state shown while query loads.

Playwright admin-audit.spec.ts: after performing at least one template admin action (create or publish), admin navigates to /admin/audit; verifies the table shows at least one entry with the expected Action (e.g. TEMPLATE_CREATED or TEMPLATE_PUBLISHED) and the correct Actor email; clicks the entry row and verifies the Drawer opens displaying the JSON metadata payload.
tasks.decision: Inferred: admin/auditLog/list query is not listed in tech spec §7.1 but is required for the audit log page. Implemented in T41 alongside the other admin read-only queries as a simple requireAdmin-gated query returning all auditLog rows.

tasks.title: Landing page frontend (src/routes/LandingPage.tsx)
tasks.summary: Build the logged-out landing page at / per design doc §4.1: hero with tagline, three-step explainer (Private Coaching → Shared Conversation → Resolution), privacy section, footer with ToS and privacy links. Logged-in users are redirected to /dashboard. No testimonials, no aggressive CTAs, no pricing.
acceptance_criteria: / renders the landing page for logged-out users and redirects to /dashboard for logged-in users.
Hero section: single-sentence tagline ('A calm place to work through a difficult conversation.'), short subhead, 'Start a case' primary CTA (routes to /login then /cases/new).
Three-step explainer section: Private Coaching → Shared Conversation → Resolution, minimal iconographic layout.
Privacy section: 'Your words are yours. Here's how we protect them.' with link to privacy policy.
Footer: Terms, Privacy, Contact links.
NO: testimonials, pricing, repeated aggressive CTAs.
Page uses --bg-canvas background (#FAF8F5) and Inter font per design doc §2.1-2.2.
Color contrast meets WCAG AA for all text elements.

tasks.title: Accessibility implementation pass (keyboard navigation, aria, focus management, WCAG AA audit)
tasks.summary: Conduct and implement the accessibility pass described in PRD §5.4 and design doc §7. Covers: keyboard-only critical path verification, aria-live regions for all chat views, focus management on modal open/close and route changes, color contrast audit using Playwright accessibility snapshots, and prefers-reduced-motion implementation across all animated elements.
acceptance_criteria: A keyboard-only run of the critical path (login → create case → private coaching → joint chat → closure) completes without requiring a mouse.
All modals (closure modal, privacy 'learn more' modal, mark-complete confirmation) trap focus on open and restore focus to trigger on close.
Route change: on each navigation, focus moves to the page's primary <h1> element.
Draft Coach panel: on open, focus moves to the textarea.
Chat regions across PrivateCoachingView, JointChatView, and DraftCoachPanel use role='log' and aria-live='polite'.
Streaming messages: an aria-live region announces 'Coach is replying' once; character-by-character updates do not trigger additional announcements.
prefers-reduced-motion: streaming cursor blink, route crossfades, message fade-ins, and coaching completion animations are disabled when OS setting is enabled.
Playwright accessibility.spec.ts: runs axe-core or Playwright's built-in accessibility checks on all major pages and asserts zero critical violations.
tasks.decision: Inferred: accessibility.spec.ts is not explicitly listed in tech spec §10.1 but is required by PRD §5.4 (WCAG 2.1 AA target) and design doc §7.3 ('verified via Playwright accessibility snapshot'). Named separately to ensure it is a first-class deliverable rather than a verbal commitment.

tasks.title: Joint chat messages Convex query (jointChat/messages)
tasks.summary: Implement the reactive jointChat/messages Convex query that returns all JointMessages for a case, gated to parties in the appropriate phase. This is the real-time subscription used by JointChatView (T26) and the read-only ClosedCaseView transcript (T31).
acceptance_criteria: jointChat/messages({ caseId }) is guarded by requireParty; throws CONFLICT if case status is DRAFT_PRIVATE_COACHING or BOTH_PRIVATE_COACHING (joint chat not yet open).
Returns all JointMessages for the case ordered by createdAt asc.
Non-party caller returns FORBIDDEN.
The query is reactive (uses ctx.db.query); both parties' clients receive real-time push updates when new JointMessages are inserted via Convex reactive channel.
convex-test unit tests: non-party caller returns FORBIDDEN; query in DRAFT_PRIVATE_COACHING state throws CONFLICT; both parties receive the same message list.

tasks.title: Draft Coach CRUD Convex mutations (startSession, sendMessage, sendFinalDraft, discardSession)
tasks.summary: Implement the four Draft Coach write operations: draftCoach/startSession (creates draftSessions row), draftCoach/sendMessage (inserts draftMessages row, schedules generateResponse), draftCoach/sendFinalDraft (reads finalDraft, posts to joint chat, marks session SENT), and draftCoach/discardSession (marks session DISCARDED without sending). The AI generation action is in T28; the session query is in T39.
acceptance_criteria: draftCoach/startSession({ caseId }) is guarded by requireParty; creates a draftSessions row with status=ACTIVE and returns the sessionId.
draftCoach/sendMessage({ sessionId, content }) inserts a draftMessages row (role=USER) and schedules draftCoach/generateResponse (T28) action.

draftCoach/sendFinalDraft({ sessionId }) reads draftSessions.finalDraft; calls ctx.runMutation(api.jointChat.sendUserMessage, ...) to post the draft text to joint chat; marks draftSessions.status=SENT.
draftCoach/discardSession({ sessionId }) marks status=DISCARDED. No message is posted to joint chat.
All mutations are guarded by requireParty; a user can only operate on sessions belonging to their own draftSessions row.
convex-test unit tests: discardSession does not post to joint chat; sendFinalDraft posts exactly one joint chat message; sendFinalDraft on an already-SENT session throws CONFLICT.

tasks.title: Draft Coach session Convex query (draftCoach/session)
tasks.summary: Implement the reactive draftCoach/session Convex query that returns the caller's active draft session and its messages. Used by DraftCoachPanel (T29) to subscribe to real-time draft conversation updates. The session is private to the drafting user — the other party cannot see it.
acceptance_criteria: draftCoach/session({ caseId }) is guarded by requireParty and returns { session: DraftSession, messages: DraftMessage[] } for the caller's ACTIVE session, or null if no active session exists.
The query never returns another party's draft session data: a convex-test test verifies that querying as the non-drafting party returns null.
The query is reactive; the DraftCoachPanel UI receives push updates as draftMessages are inserted.
convex-test unit tests: non-party caller returns FORBIDDEN; calling party sees their own ACTIVE session; other party sees null for the same caseId.

tasks.title: CLOSED_ABANDONED Convex cron job (convex/crons.ts)
tasks.summary: Implement the Convex daily cron job that scans JOINT_ACTIVE cases for 30-day inactivity and transitions them to CLOSED_ABANDONED. Runs automatically via Convex's built-in scheduler. The closure mutations (proposeClosure, confirmClosure, unilateralClose) are in T30.
acceptance_criteria: convex/crons.ts defines a daily cron (using Convex's cronJobs API) that queries JOINT_ACTIVE cases with updatedAt < now - 30 days.
For each stale case, calls transitionCase(JOINT_ACTIVE, CLOSED_ABANDONED) via the T4 state machine and updates cases.status and cases.closedAt.
Cases updated within the last 30 days are untouched by the cron.
convex-test unit tests: cron transitions only stale JOINT_ACTIVE cases; a JOINT_ACTIVE case with updatedAt 29 days ago is not transitioned; a case with updatedAt 31 days ago is transitioned.
tasks.decision: Inferred: a Convex cron job for CLOSED_ABANDONED requires persisting updatedAt on the cases table. The schema includes updatedAt (v.number()); the cron queries by this field. Index not specified in tech spec §3.1 for this query; adding an implicit scan is acceptable at v1 scale (≤50 concurrent cases).

tasks.title: Admin template and audit log Convex queries (listAll, templateVersions/list, templates/list, auditLog/list)
tasks.summary: Implement four admin-surface Convex queries from tech spec §7.1: admin/templates/listAll (all templates including archived), admin/templateVersions/list (all versions for a template), templates/list (non-archived templates for the category picker), and admin/auditLog/list (all audit log entries). These are the read layer consumed by the admin frontend (T33) and audit log page (T34).
acceptance_criteria: admin/templates/listAll({}) is guarded by requireAdmin; returns all templates including archived, ordered by createdAt desc.
admin/templateVersions/list({ templateId }) is guarded by requireAdmin; returns all versions for the template in version order.
templates/list({ category? }) is guarded by requireAuth; returns only non-archived templates (archivedAt is null); supports optional category filter.
admin/auditLog/list({}) is guarded by requireAdmin; returns all auditLog rows ordered by createdAt desc.

convex-test unit tests: admin/templates/listAll as non-admin throws FORBIDDEN; templates/list excludes archived templates; admin/auditLog/list as non-admin throws FORBIDDEN; templateVersions/list returns versions in correct order.
tasks.decision: Inferred: admin/auditLog/list is not listed in tech spec §7.1 but is logically required by the audit log page (T34). Grouped here with the other admin read-only queries for cohesion.

tasks.title: Private coaching AI response Convex action (privateCoaching/generateAIResponse)
tasks.summary: Implement the privateCoaching/generateAIResponse Convex action scheduled by privateCoaching/sendUserMessage (T18). Assembles the private coaching prompt via assemblePrompt(PRIVATE_COACH) (T7), compresses context via compressIfNeeded (T6), calls Claude Sonnet 4.5 with streaming, updates the AI message row as tokens arrive, applies the cost cap check, and handles retry and error state logic. Strict per-user isolation: every DB access in this action filters by caseId AND userId.
acceptance_criteria: privateCoaching/generateAIResponse({ caseId, userId }) is an action (not mutation); it calls assemblePrompt with role=PRIVATE_COACH (T7) and uses compressIfNeeded (T6) for context window management.
Inserts an AI message row (role=AI, status=STREAMING) at the start, then updates its content as tokens arrive in ~50ms batches via ctx.runMutation; sets status=COMPLETE on finish.
All privateMessages queries in this action filter by caseId AND userId — the action never reads another party's messages even when assembling context.
AI retry: on 429 or network timeout (>30s), retries once with 2s exponential backoff; on second failure sets message status=ERROR and stops.
Cost cap check: before calling Sonnet, calls checkCostCap (T6); if 'hard', inserts a cost-limited AI message row (status=COMPLETE) and does not call the API.
convex-test unit tests: action with userId=partyA does not read partyB's privateMessages during context assembly; simulated 429 triggers retry; second failure transitions message to ERROR status.
Playwright privacy.spec.ts: as User B, attempt to query User A's private messages via direct Convex client call — must return FORBIDDEN.

tasks.title: Synthesis result Convex query (jointChat/mySynthesis)
tasks.summary: Implement the reactive jointChat/mySynthesis Convex query that returns the calling party's synthesisText after synthesis/generate (T23) has populated it. Used by ReadyForJointView (T24) for the synthesis display card, by JointChatView's 'My guidance' link (T26), and by ClosedCaseView's My Guidance tab (T31). Strictly private: the query returns only the caller's own synthesisText and never the other party's.
acceptance_criteria: jointChat/mySynthesis({ caseId }) is guarded by requireParty and returns only the calling user's partyStates.synthesisText (never the other party's).
Returns null when synthesisText has not yet been generated (synthesis/generate action has not yet written the field).
The query is reactive (uses ctx.db.query); ReadyForJointView and JointChatView clients receive push updates as soon as synthesisText is written by the synthesis/generate action (T23).
convex-test unit tests: mySynthesis called by party A returns only party A's synthesisText; called by party B returns only party B's synthesisText; a non-party caller receives FORBIDDEN.
TypeScript compiles cleanly; return type is { synthesisText: string } | null.

stack_rules.convex:
## Convex

Convex is the all-in-one backend for Conflict Coach: it provides the document database, reactive query subscriptions, transactional mutations, non-transactional actions (for external API calls), built-in auth, and a cron scheduler. **Every piece of server-side state lives in Convex.** There is no separate HTTP server, no REST API, and no Express layer — function calls are the API.

### Implied / companion libraries
- `convex` (npm) — the core SDK; includes `convex/server`, `convex/values`, and `convex/react`
- `convex-test` — in-memory Convex runtime for unit tests (used for all query/mutation/action tests)
- `@convex-dev/auth` — Convex Auth library (magic link + Google OAuth)

### Best practices

**Always start every function with identity check:**
```ts
// convex/lib/auth.ts pattern — used as the first line in every query/mutation/action
const identity = await ctx.auth.getUserIdentity();
if (!identity) throw new ConvexError({ code: "UNAUTHENTICATED", message: "Not authenticated" });
```

**Map logic correctly to function types:**
- `query` — reactive read; use for dashboard lists, chat messages, case status. Subscribe via `useQuery`.
- `mutation` — transactional write; use for sending a message row, state transitions, token redemption. Cannot call external APIs.
- `action` — non-transactional; the only type that can call Claude or any external API. Calls mutations internally via `ctx.runMutation` to persist results.

**Schema is the source of truth; enforce it via `convex/schema.ts`:**
```ts
import { defineSchema, defineTable } from "convex/server";
import { v } from "convex/values";
export default defineSchema({
  cases: defineTable({
    status: v.union(v.literal("DRAFT_PRIVATE_COACHING"), ...),
    schemaVersion: v.literal(1),
    isSolo: v.boolean(),
  }).index("by_initiator", ["initiatorUserId"]),
});
```

**Use `ctx.db.query` for reactive reads (not `ctx.db.get` in a loop):**
```ts
// ✅ correct — reactive; client auto-updates on table change
const messages = await ctx.db
  .query("privateMessages")
  .withIndex("by_case_and_user", (q) => q.eq("caseId", caseId).eq("userId", callerId))
  .collect();

// ❌ wrong — non-reactive pattern, misses live updates
const ids = [id1, id2];
const msgs = await Promise.all(ids.map(id => ctx.db.get(id)));
```

**Schedule actions from mutations, not from other actions:**
```ts
// Inside a mutation:
await ctx.scheduler.runAfter(0, api.privateCoaching.generateAIResponse, { caseId, userId });
```

### Anti-patterns

- **Calling external APIs inside a mutation** — mutations are transactional and cannot call `fetch` or any async external service. Put all API calls in an `action`.
- **Reading another party's private data without explicit filter** — every `privateMessages` query MUST filter by `userId == callerUserId`. Omitting the filter leaks cross-party data.
- **Trusting client-provided role claims** — admin authorization must call `requireAdmin(ctx)` server-side on every invocation. Never read a role from request parameters.
- **Using `v.any()` in schema fields** — use it only for the `auditLog.metadata` field; everywhere else use typed value validators.

### Architectural conventions
- All Convex functions live under `convex/`. Naming: `<domain>/<action>.ts` (e.g., `cases/create.ts`, `privateCoaching/generateAIResponse.ts`).
- Shared helpers live in `convex/lib/` (auth.ts, errors.ts, stateMachine.ts, prompts.ts, privacyFilter.ts, compression.ts).
- The React client never accesses another party's data; the server enforces all boundaries.
- Cron jobs live in `convex/crons.ts`; use `cronJobs.daily(...)` for the 30-day abandoned-case sweep.

### Testing strategy
- **Unit tests (convex-test):** wrap every query/mutation/action test in the in-memory Convex runtime. Do NOT mock `ctx.db` manually — use `convex-test`'s real in-memory implementation.
- **Pure utility modules** (errors.ts, privacyFilter.ts, prompts.ts) are tested with plain Vitest (no Convex ctx needed).
- Test all auth boundary cases: unauthenticated caller, authenticated non-party caller, correct party.

### Common pitfalls
- **`useQuery` vs `useMutation` return shapes:** `useQuery` returns `undefined` while loading (not `null`). Guard with `if (data === undefined) return <Skeleton />`.
- **Scheduling in actions:** you cannot use `ctx.scheduler` inside an `action`; scheduling must happen in a `mutation` triggered before or after the action completes.
- **Reactive update storm during streaming:** updating a row ~20 times/second during AI streaming triggers 20 reactive pushes per client. This is acceptable at v1 scale; do not "fix" it with polling.
- **Index mismatches:** queries that use `.withIndex` must match the exact index definition in schema.ts. A typo in the index name causes a runtime error, not a compile error.

### Configuration notes
- `VITE_CONVEX_URL` is the client-side env var pointing to the Convex deployment.
- `npx convex dev` starts the local dev tunnel and hot-deploys functions on save.
- Environment variables for backend functions (ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, etc.) are set via `npx convex env set KEY value`, NOT in `.env.local`.
stack_rules.convex-auth:
## Convex Auth

Convex Auth (`@convex-dev/auth`) is the authentication layer for Conflict Coach, providing magic-link (email OTP) and Google OAuth flows integrated directly into Convex. It supplies `ctx.auth.getUserIdentity()` in every Convex function, manages sessions, and handles the OAuth redirect lifecycle. The project maintains its own `users` table keyed by email so the data model is provider-agnostic; Convex Auth supplies the identity, and we upsert on first login.

### Implied / companion libraries
- `@convex-dev/auth`
- Resend (or similar) as the email sending provider for magic links
- Google OAuth client credentials (configured in Convex env vars)

### Best practices

**Upsert on first login — never assume the user row exists:**
```ts
export async function getUserByEmail(ctx: QueryCtx | MutationCtx, email: string) {
  const existing = await ctx.db
    .query("users")
    .withIndex("by_email", (q) => q.eq("email", email))
    .unique();
  if (existing) return existing;
  // First login: create user row
  const id = await ctx.db.insert("users", {
    email,
    role: "USER",
    createdAt: Date.now(),
  });
  return ctx.db.get(id);
}
```

**Keep the invite token in the URL through the OAuth round-trip:** When the user visits `/invite/:token` unauthenticated and clicks Google OAuth, store the token in `localStorage` keyed by a stable key. On return from OAuth, read it back and resume the accept flow.

**Never promote to ADMIN via client input:** Admin promotion in v1 is a manual Convex Dashboard action. There is no self-serve admin claim. Every admin check is `user.role === "ADMIN"` read from the database, not from a JWT claim.

### Anti-patterns
- **Trusting the identity's email claim without upserting the users row** — always call `getUserByEmail` which upserts; never use `ctx.auth.getUserIdentity().email` as a key into private data without confirming the row exists.
- **Sharing session tokens between parties in solo mode** — both parties share the same User record in solo mode; the party distinction lives in `partyStates.role`, not in the auth session.

### Architectural conventions
- Auth config lives in `convex/auth.ts` and is imported in `convex/http.ts` for route registration.
- `requireAuth(ctx)` in `convex/lib/auth.ts` is the single call site for identity extraction in all Convex functions.
- The swap-out plan (Clerk) requires no data model change — only `auth.ts` changes.

### Testing strategy
- Unit tests mock `ctx.auth.getUserIdentity()` to return a fake identity object (convex-test supports this).
- Playwright e2e uses magic-link mock email capture (intercept the outbound email request with a test stub) rather than the real Resend API.

### Common pitfalls
- **Convex Auth is in beta** (as of April 2026). Unexpected edge cases in the magic-link flow are possible. If a bug blocks delivery, Clerk is the escape hatch — no DB migration needed.
- **Session expiry is 30 days of inactivity** (Convex Auth default). No custom expiry code is needed. Do not add a manual session expiry layer that conflicts with this.
- **`SITE_URL` env var** must be set in Convex env for invite link generation and magic-link redirect URLs.
stack_rules.react:
## React 18

React 18 is the frontend view layer. Combined with the Convex React client (`useQuery`, `useMutation`, `useAction`), it provides a reactive UI that auto-updates when Convex data changes. The app is a single-page application (SPA) served by Vite, with no server-side rendering.

### Implied / companion libraries
- `react-dom`
- `react-router-dom` v6 (SPA routing)
- `@tanstack/react-query` is NOT used — Convex hooks replace it entirely

### Best practices

**Use `useQuery` for all data reads; treat `undefined` as loading:**
```tsx
const messages = useQuery(api.privateCoaching.myMessages, { caseId });
if (messages === undefined) return <ChatSkeleton />;  // loading
if (messages.length === 0) return <EmptyState />;
return <MessageList messages={messages} />;
```

**Wrap the app in `ConvexProvider` and `ConvexAuthProvider` at the root:**
```tsx
// src/main.tsx
const convex = new ConvexReactClient(import.meta.env.VITE_CONVEX_URL);
<ConvexProvider client={convex}>
  <ConvexAuthProvider>
    <App />
  </ConvexAuthProvider>
</ConvexProvider>
```

**Use optimistic updates for user-sent messages to avoid perceived lag:**
```tsx
const sendMessage = useMutation(api.jointChat.sendUserMessage).withOptimisticUpdate(
  (localStore, args) => {
    const messages = localStore.getQuery(api.jointChat.messages, { caseId: args.caseId });
    if (messages) {
      localStore.setQuery(api.jointChat.messages, { caseId: args.caseId }, [
        ...messages,
        { _id: "optimistic", content: args.content, authorType: "USER", status: "COMPLETE", createdAt: Date.now() },
      ]);
    }
  }
);
```

### Anti-patterns
- **Storing Convex-sourced data in local React state** — leads to stale state. Read directly from `useQuery`; the reactive subscription keeps it fresh.
- **Calling `useMutation` result directly inside `useEffect`** — triggers infinite loops. Call mutations in event handlers, not effects.
- **Rendering the other party's private transcript** — client code must never receive or hold the other party's private messages. If an API returns unexpected cross-party data, treat it as a bug.

### Architectural conventions
- Route pages live in `src/routes/`. Shared components live in `src/components/`. Each subdirectory is one concern: `chat/`, `draft/`, `layout/`, `ui/`.
- Protected routes redirect unauthenticated users to `/login` via a `ProtectedRoute` wrapper component.
- Solo mode party toggle is URL-state (`?as=initiator|invitee`), not React state, so it survives refresh.

### Testing strategy
- **Vitest + @testing-library/react** for component unit tests. Test rendering, interaction, and prop-driven output.
- **Playwright** for full E2E flows. Components are tested at the page level in E2E tests; unit tests cover isolated component behavior.
- Mock Convex hooks in unit tests using `vi.mock('convex/react')`.

### Common pitfalls
- **`useQuery` returns `undefined` on first render** — always handle the loading state. Rendering `undefined.map(...)` crashes.
- **Stale closures in `useMutation` callbacks** — capture latest values from refs or use the mutation result's returned function, not a closure from an old render cycle.
- **`aria-live` pollution during streaming** — do NOT put `aria-live` on every streaming token update. Announce "Coach is replying" once when `status === "STREAMING"` starts, then stop. Use a separate off-screen element for the announcement.
stack_rules.vite:
## Vite

Vite is the build tool and dev server for the React SPA. It provides near-instant HMR, TypeScript transpilation, and production bundling. There is no Webpack or CRA in this stack.

### Implied / companion libraries
- `vite` (core)
- `@vitejs/plugin-react` (React fast-refresh + JSX transform)
- TypeScript (transpiled by Vite's esbuild integration)

### Best practices
- **Use `import.meta.env.VITE_*` for all client-side env vars.** Any variable not prefixed `VITE_` is invisible to the browser bundle.
- **`.env.local`** holds local-dev values (never committed). `.env.example` documents required keys.
- `vite.config.ts` should proxy `/api` to Convex in development if needed (though Convex's WebSocket client connects directly without a proxy).

### Anti-patterns
- **Putting secrets in `VITE_*` variables** — any `VITE_*` value is bundled into the JS and visible in the browser. `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` must only live in Convex environment variables, never in Vite env.
- **Using `require()` syntax** — Vite is ESM-only. Use `import`.

### Architectural conventions
- `vite.config.ts` at the repo root configures the single SPA build.
- `index.html` is the SPA entry point; React mounts into `<div id="root">`.
- Build output goes to `dist/` for production deployment.

### Common pitfalls
- **Cold-start sequence:** `VITE_CONVEX_URL` must be populated from `npx convex dev` output before `npm run dev` is started. Running `npm run dev` before Convex is initialized gives a runtime error about missing `VITE_CONVEX_URL`.
stack_rules.typescript:
## TypeScript

TypeScript is used throughout: the React frontend, all Convex backend functions, and all utility modules are typed. The compiler is configured in strict mode. Convex's schema-derived types flow end-to-end from `convex/schema.ts` through to the React components via `Doc<"tableName">` generics.

### Implied / companion libraries
- `typescript` (devDependency)
- `tsconfig.json` with `strict: true`, `moduleResolution: "bundler"`, `target: "ES2022"`

### Best practices

**Use Convex's `Doc` and `Id` generics for table types — never hand-write parallel interfaces:**
```ts
import type { Doc, Id } from "./_generated/dataModel";

type Case = Doc<"cases">;                // Convex-generated type from schema
type CaseId = Id<"cases">;              // Branded ID type

function getCase(ctx: QueryCtx, id: CaseId): Promise<Case | null> {
  return ctx.db.get(id);
}
```

**Derive `CaseStatus` from the schema union, not from a hand-written enum:**
```ts
// convex/lib/stateMachine.ts
import type { Doc } from "../_generated/dataModel";
type CaseStatus = Doc<"cases">["status"];  // derived from schema — stays in sync automatically
```

**All Convex function argument and return types use `v.*` validators for runtime safety:**
```ts
export const myMessages = query({
  args: { caseId: v.id("cases") },
  returns: v.array(v.object({ content: v.string(), role: v.union(v.literal("USER"), v.literal("AI")) })),
  handler: async (ctx, { caseId }) => { ... },
});
```

### Anti-patterns
- **`any` types** — never acceptable without explicit justification and a comment explaining why. Use `unknown` + type narrowing instead.
- **Parallel hand-crafted interfaces that duplicate schema fields** — schema → `Doc<>` is the source of truth. Duplicating creates drift.
- **Ignoring `undefined` return from `useQuery`** — TypeScript will catch this if `strict: true` is set; do not suppress the warning.

### Architectural conventions
- `tsconfig.json` at root; Vite uses `tsconfig.app.json` for the frontend.
- Convex generates types into `convex/_generated/` on `npx convex dev` — never edit those files manually.
- CI runs `tsc --noEmit` as the `typecheck` job; zero type errors required to merge.

### Common pitfalls
- **`_generated/` types lag behind schema changes** during development — run `npx convex dev` to regenerate before TypeScript-checking.
- **Forgetting `v.optional()` in schema** causes Convex to treat absent fields as validation errors rather than `undefined`. Use `v.optional(v.string())` for nullable optional fields.
stack_rules.shadcn-ui:
## shadcn/ui

shadcn/ui provides the accessible, unstyled Radix UI primitives styled with Tailwind. In Conflict Coach it covers: Dialog (modals), Button, Input, Textarea, Skeleton (loading states), Card, and Tabs. Components are copied into `src/components/ui/` and owned by the project — they are not an npm package dependency.

### Implied / companion libraries
- `@radix-ui/*` (peer deps installed by shadcn add)
- `class-variance-authority` (cva — for variant-based class composition)
- `clsx` + `tailwind-merge` (cn utility)
- `lucide-react` (icon set)

### Best practices

**Use the `cn` utility for conditional class merging:**
```ts
import { cn } from "@/lib/utils";
<Button className={cn("w-full", isLoading && "opacity-50 cursor-not-allowed")} />
```

**Never override Radix focus-visible styles** — they are the keyboard navigation mechanism. If you need custom focus rings, extend the Radix class, don't remove it.

**Modals must trap focus and restore on close** — Radix Dialog handles this automatically. Do not build custom modal divs.

### Anti-patterns
- **Building custom modal, dropdown, or tooltip components from scratch** — Radix primitives handle all the ARIA roles, keyboard navigation, and focus trapping. Rebuilding them introduces accessibility regressions.
- **Modifying the files in `src/components/ui/` with project-specific logic** — keep shadcn primitives pure; compose them in feature components.

### Testing strategy
- Component tests verify that shadcn-based UI is keyboard accessible using `@testing-library/user-event` tab/enter sequences.
- Do not test Radix internals — test that the composed feature component opens, focuses correctly, and closes.

### Common pitfalls
- **Skeleton component during `useQuery` loading** — always render `<Skeleton />` when `useQuery` returns `undefined`, not an empty array. The two cases have different meanings.
- **shadcn Dialog's `onOpenChange` prop** — failing to wire this up causes the modal to be uncloseable on Escape key.
stack_rules.eslint-prettier:
## ESLint + Prettier

ESLint enforces code quality rules; Prettier enforces consistent formatting. Both run in CI as the first job. The project requires zero warnings (`--max-warnings 0`) to merge.

### Implied / companion libraries
- `eslint`
- `@typescript-eslint/eslint-plugin` + `@typescript-eslint/parser`
- `eslint-plugin-react` + `eslint-plugin-react-hooks`
- `prettier`
- `eslint-config-prettier` (disables ESLint rules that conflict with Prettier)

### Best practices
- Run `eslint --fix` and `prettier --write` before every commit.
- Configure `eslint-plugin-react-hooks` to enforce exhaustive-deps — missing deps in useEffect/useMemo are a common source of stale closure bugs.

### Anti-patterns
- **Inline `eslint-disable` comments** — acceptable only for external SDK type incompatibilities with a documented reason. Never to silence a legitimate warning.
- **Skipping Prettier formatting** — inconsistent formatting causes noisy diffs and makes code review harder.

### Common pitfalls
- **ESLint and TypeScript version mismatch** — `@typescript-eslint` versions must be compatible with the installed `typescript` version. Check the compatibility matrix when upgrading either.
stack_rules.github-actions:
## GitHub Actions

GitHub Actions runs the four-job CI pipeline: `lint` → `typecheck` → `unit` → `e2e`. All four must pass before a PR can merge (branch protection rule on `main`).

### Implied / companion libraries
- `.github/workflows/ci.yml`
- GitHub repository secrets: `VITE_CONVEX_URL`, `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` (test key), `GOOGLE_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID`, `GOOGLE_OAUTH_CLIENT_SECRET`

### Best practices

**Job ordering with `needs`:**
```yaml
jobs:
  lint:     { ... }
  typecheck: { needs: lint }
  unit:     { needs: typecheck }
  e2e:      { needs: unit }
```

**Use `CLAUDE_MOCK=true` for the E2E job** to avoid real Anthropic API calls in CI.

**Cache `node_modules`** to speed up runs:
```yaml
- uses: actions/cache@v3
  with:
    path: ~/.npm
    key: ${{ runner.os }}-node-${{ hashFiles('**/package-lock.json') }}
```

### Anti-patterns
- **Committing secrets to the repository** — all sensitive values must be GitHub Actions secrets, not hardcoded in workflow YAML.
- **Running E2E against production Convex deployment** — CI should use a dedicated dev/test Convex deployment.

### Common pitfalls
- **Playwright browser installation** — add `npx playwright install --with-deps` as a CI step before running E2E tests. Playwright does not bundle browser binaries.
- **Convex deployment not ready before E2E** — the Convex dev deployment used in CI must be pre-deployed (not started on-demand per run) or the E2E job must include a step to run `npx convex deploy`.

design_rules.tokens:
## Color Tokens

All color values are declared as CSS custom properties and MUST be consumed via token names. Never reference hex values directly in component code. The design system has a default warm-neutral theme plus two named variants ("slate" and "earth") toggled via `data-variant` on `<html>`.

### Surface & Text Tokens (light / dark)
| Token | Light | Dark | Role |
|---|---|---|---|
| `--bg-canvas` | `#FAF8F5` | `#1A1816` | Page background — warm off-white |
| `--bg-surface` | `#FFFFFF` | `#242220` | Cards, chat bubbles, panels |
| `--bg-surface-subtle` | `#F3EFE9` | `#2E2B28` | Hover states, subtle section fills |
| `--text-primary` | `#1F1D1A` | `#F2EFE9` | Body text, headings |
| `--text-secondary` | `#5C5952` | `#A8A39A` | Labels, metadata, helper text |
| `--text-tertiary` | `#8A8680` | `#7A766E` | Timestamps, hints, placeholders |
| `--border-default` | `#E5E0D8` | `#3A3632` | Hairline borders, dividers |
| `--border-strong` | `#CBC4B8` | `#4A4640` | Input borders, strong dividers |

### Accent Tokens (sage green — primary action)
| Token | Light | Dark |
|---|---|---|
| `--accent` | `#6B8E7F` | `#89A99B` |
| `--accent-hover` | `#5A7A6C` | `#9ABAAC` |
| `--accent-subtle` | `#DCE7E0` | `#2E3A35` |
| `--accent-on` | `#FFFFFF` | `#1A1816` |

### Semantic / Role Tokens
| Token | Light | Dark | Role |
|---|---|---|---|
| `--coach-accent` | `#8B7AB5` | `#A797CC` | Coach AI identity — dusty lavender |
| `--coach-subtle` | `#EAE4F2` | `#342E42` | Coach bubble backgrounds |
| `--party-initiator` | `#6B85A8` | `#8BA3C2` | Initiator avatar & bubble fill |
| `--party-initiator-subtle` | `#DFE5EF` | `#2C3542` | Initiator bubble background |
| `--party-invitee` | `#B07A8F` | `#CC96A9` | Invitee avatar & bubble fill |
| `--party-invitee-subtle` | `#EFE0E4` | `#3E2E34` | Invitee bubble background |
| `--danger` | `#B5594D` | `#CC786D` | Destructive actions — muted terracotta |
| `--danger-subtle` | `#F2DCD8` | `#3E2A27` | Danger backgrounds |
| `--warning` | `#B58B4D` | `#CC9F6D` | Inflammatory-content highlight |
| `--warning-subtle` | `#F2E5D4` | `#3E3228` | Warning backgrounds |
| `--private-tint` | `#F0E9E0` | `#2D2924` | "This is private to you" panel backgrounds |

### Anti-patterns
- **Never** write inline hex or RGB values in components. Always reference `var(--token-name)`.
- **Never** invent new color values not listed above. If none fits, surface it for design review.
- Dark mode is achieved by setting `data-theme="dark"` on `<html>`. The same token names resolve to their dark counterparts automatically — no component needs to branch on theme.

## Radius & Shadow Tokens
| Token | Value |
|---|---|
| `--radius-sm` | `6px` |
| `--radius-md` | `10px` |
| `--radius-lg` | `14px` |
| `--radius-xl` | `20px` |
| `--radius-full` | `9999px` |
| `--shadow-0` | `none` |
| `--shadow-1` | `0 1px 2px rgba(0,0,0,0.04)` |
| `--shadow-2` | `0 4px 12px rgba(0,0,0,0.06)` |
| `--shadow-3` | `0 12px 32px rgba(0,0,0,0.10)` |

Cards rest at `shadow-1`. Popovers/dropdowns use `shadow-2`. Modals and the Draft Coach panel use `shadow-3`.
design_rules.typography:
## Font Families
- **Sans:** `Inter` (variable, Google Fonts) — weights 400, 500, 600 only. Loaded via `--font-sans: "Inter", system-ui, -apple-system, "Segoe UI", Roboto, sans-serif`.
- **Mono:** `JetBrains Mono` — weights 400, 500. Used for invite links, audit log IDs, token names in UI. Loaded via `--font-mono: "JetBrains Mono", ui-monospace, SFMono-Regular, Menlo, Consolas, monospace`.

## Type Scale
| Role | Size | Line-height | Weight | Token / class |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Page heading (H1) | 40px | 48px | 500 | `letter-spacing: -0.02em` |
| Section heading (H2) | 28px | 36px | 500 | `letter-spacing: -0.015em` |
| Sub-heading (H3/H4) | 15–16px | default | 500 | no tracking |
| Body | 15px | 1.6 | 400 | base font-size of `<body>` |
| Chat bubble body | 16px | 1.55 | 400 | slightly larger for readability |
| Label | 14px | auto | 500 | form labels |
| Helper / caption | 13px | 1.5 | 400 | `color: var(--text-secondary)` |
| Timestamp / hint | 12px | auto | 400 | `color: var(--text-tertiary)` |
| Mono kicker | 11px | auto | 400 | `letter-spacing: 0.08em; text-transform: uppercase` |
| Mono tag / meta | 11–12px | auto | 400 | mono family |

## Key Conventions
- Headings use **weight 500, not 700**. This is a deliberate choice — the app context is stressful and bold headings feel aggressive. Never set a heading to 600 or 700 unless it is truly a marketing/hero element.
- Body font-size is 15px globally. Chat bubbles bump to 16px for readability.
- Chat timestamps are 11–12px in `--text-tertiary`.
- Letter-spacing on all headings is negative (`-0.01em` to `-0.02em`). Never positive on body text.
- `-webkit-font-smoothing: antialiased` is set on `<body>` — do not override this.
- Anti-patterns: never use Inter at weight 700 in application chrome. Never use a sans font for invite link values or audit IDs — those must be `--font-mono`.
design_rules.component-patterns:
## shadcn/ui as the Primitive Layer
All UI primitives come from shadcn/ui. The canonical primitives for this project are:
`Button`, `Input`, `Textarea`, `Dialog`, `Sheet`, `Tooltip`, `Select`, `RadioGroup`, `Card`, `Toast`.

Never write a custom modal from scratch — use shadcn's `Dialog`. Never write a custom side panel — use shadcn's `Sheet`. Extend primitives by composing them with project-specific CSS custom properties; do not fork the shadcn component source unless absolutely necessary.

## Button Variants
Four variants are defined. Map them as follows:

| Variant | CSS class / intent | When to use |
|---|---|---|
| Primary | `btn-primary` — background `var(--accent)`, text `var(--accent-on)` | The single primary action per screen (e.g. "Send magic link", "Enter Joint Session", "Send this message") |
| Secondary | `btn-secondary` — white bg, border `var(--border-strong)` | Alternative or supporting actions (e.g. "Continue Coaching", "Edit before sending") |
| Ghost | `btn-ghost` — transparent bg, hover `var(--bg-surface-subtle)` | Tertiary actions, nav items, icon-only actions |
| Danger | `btn-danger` — background `var(--danger)`, white text | Destructive actions only: "Close without resolution", "Archive Template" |

Sizes: default (`padding: 10px 16px`), `btn-sm` (`6px 12px`), `btn-lg` (`14px 24px`), `btn-icon` (`8px`, square 36×36px for icon-only).

Border-radius on buttons is `--radius-md` (10px). Transition duration is `--dur-fast` (150ms) with `--ease-out`.

**One primary button per screen** — this is a hard design constraint (DesignDoc §1.4). If you find yourself placing two `btn-primary` elements at the same hierarchy level, one must be demoted to secondary.

## Form Controls
- All inputs and textareas use `border: 1px solid var(--border-strong)`, `border-radius: var(--radius-md)`, `background: var(--bg-surface)`.
- Focus state: `border-color: var(--accent)` + `box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--accent-subtle)`. No `outline` — the box-shadow ring IS the focus ring.
- Placeholder text: `color: var(--text-tertiary)`.
- Labels: 14px / weight 500, `margin-bottom: 6px`.
- Helper text: 13px / `var(--text-secondary)`, `margin-top: 6px`.
- Error text: 13px / `var(--danger)`, `margin-top: 6px`.
- Textareas have `min-height: 88px` and `resize: vertical` by default.

## Radio Cards (Category Picker)
The case-creation category step uses radio cards (`radio-card`), not a standard dropdown. A selected card has a 2px `var(--accent)` border and `var(--accent-subtle)` background fill. The padding adjusts by 1px to compensate for the border width change (avoiding layout shift). Icons inside cards use `var(--accent)` color when selected, `var(--text-secondary)` when not.

## Chat Components
Three chat bubble variants exist — always choose the correct one:

| Variant | CSS class | When |
|---|---|---|
| User (self) | `bubble` (plain, right-aligned) | User's own message in any chat |
| Coach (private coaching) | `bubble-coach` — `var(--accent-subtle)` bg | AI response in private coaching |
| Coach (joint, normal) | `bubble-coach-joint` — `var(--coach-subtle)` bg + 3px left border `var(--coach-accent)` | Coach message in joint chat |
| Coach (intervention) | `bubble-coach-intervention` — same as joint but 4px left border | Coach flagging inflammatory content |
| Initiator (joint) | `bubble-party-initiator` — `var(--party-initiator-subtle)` bg | Initiator in joint chat |
| Invitee (joint) | `bubble-party-invitee` — `var(--party-invitee-subtle)` bg | Invitee in joint chat |
| Error | `bubble-error` — `var(--danger-subtle)` bg + `var(--danger)` border | Any AI error message |

Chat font-size is 16px / line-height 1.55. Bubbles are `border-radius: var(--radius-lg)`. Max-width of the chat stack is 70% of the container.

## Avatar Component
Avatars are 32×32px circles (`border-radius: var(--radius-full)`), 13px / weight 500 initials in white.
- Coach avatar: `background: var(--coach-accent)`
- Initiator avatar: `background: var(--party-initiator)`
- Invitee avatar: `background: var(--party-invitee)`
- Current user ("You"): `background: var(--accent); color: var(--accent-on)`

## Privacy Banner
The `PrivacyBanner` component wraps `background: var(--private-tint)` with a 1px bottom border and a Lucide Lock icon. Font is 13px / `var(--text-secondary)`. The banner is persistent and always rendered at the top of any screen that displays private content (private coaching view, Draft Coach panel, synthesis card). Clicking the lock icon opens a `Dialog` explaining the privacy model.

## Synthesis Card
Uses `background: var(--private-tint)`, `border: 1px solid var(--border-default)`, `border-radius: var(--radius-lg)`, `padding: 32px`. Synthesized markdown is rendered with H3 headings at 15px / weight 500 and paragraph line-height 1.7.

## Draft Coach Panel (Sheet)
Implemented as a shadcn `Sheet` sliding in from the right (420px wide, full height on desktop; full-screen bottom sheet on mobile < 768px). It uses `shadow-3`. The header always shows the Lock icon. The draft-ready card uses `background: var(--bg-surface-subtle)`, `border-radius: var(--radius-md)`, `padding: 16px`, 15px / line-height 1.6.

## Status Pills
Pills use `border-radius: var(--radius-full)`, padding `3px 10px`, font 12px / weight 500.
- "Your turn" / active: `color: var(--accent); background: var(--accent-subtle)`
- "Waiting": default subtle styling, hollow dot (border only)
- "Ready for joint": `color: var(--warning); background: var(--warning-subtle)`
- "Closed": `color: var(--text-tertiary)`, square-ish pill-dot

## Party Toggle (Solo Mode)
A segmented control with `background: var(--coach-subtle); border: 1px solid var(--coach-accent)`. The label above the toggle uses `color: var(--coach-accent); text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.05em`. Active segment: `background: var(--bg-surface); box-shadow: var(--shadow-1)`. The coach-accent color makes the solo-mode context unmistakably distinct from normal case views.

## Skeleton Screens
Use skeleton screens (not spinners) for loading states lasting > 300ms. Dashboard load skeletons show 3 case-row outlines. Never use a generic full-page spinner.
design_rules.accessibility:
## Landmarks
Every page must have exactly one `<main>` landmark. Navigation is in `<nav>`. The Draft Coach side panel is `<aside>`. Never place interactive content outside a landmark.

## Chat Regions
All chat message lists — Private Coaching, Joint Chat, Draft Coach — must be rendered in a container with `role="log"` and `aria-live="polite"`. This tells screen readers to announce new messages as they arrive without interrupting the user's current reading position.

**Streaming messages**: when an AI response begins streaming, announce `"Coach is replying"` exactly once (via an `aria-live` region update) when `status` transitions from nothing to `STREAMING`. Do NOT re-announce on every character update. When streaming completes, no announcement is needed — the full message is already in the `role="log"` region.

## Focus Management
- **Route changes**: on every client-side route transition, programmatically move focus to the page's `<h1>`. Use a `ref` + `.focus()` in a `useEffect` after navigation.
- **Modal open**: when a `Dialog` opens, focus must move to its first focusable element (shadcn's Dialog handles this via Radix `DialogPrimitive`). Focus is trapped inside the modal until it closes. On close, focus returns to the trigger element.
- **Draft Coach panel open**: focus must move to the textarea inside the panel immediately on open.
- **Phase transition**: when the user navigates to a new phase (e.g., private coaching → joint chat), focus moves to the new page's `<h1>`.

Never suppress focus outlines. The design system provides focus rings via box-shadow (see Focus Rings below).

## Focus Rings
Focus rings use `box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--accent-subtle)` combined with `border-color: var(--accent)` on form inputs. For buttons and other interactive elements, implement focus rings as:
```css
:focus-visible {
  outline: 2px solid var(--accent);
  outline-offset: 2px;
}
```
The `focus-visible` pseudo-class is mandatory — never use `:focus` alone (it triggers on mouse clicks too). Never set `outline: none` without providing an alternative visible ring.

## Keyboard Navigation
- **Enter** to send any message (all chat inputs).
- **Shift+Enter** for newline within any message textarea.
- **Escape** to close any `Dialog` or `Sheet` (shadcn/Radix handles this natively — do not override).
- All buttons are reachable via Tab / Shift+Tab in logical DOM order.
- The "Draft with Coach" button and "Send" button in the joint chat input bar must both be in the natural tab order after the message textarea.

## ARIA Labels
- Every icon-only button must have `aria-label` (e.g., `aria-label="Close Draft Coach panel"`).
- The privacy banner reads: `"Private conversation. Only you and the AI coach see this."` — set this as `aria-label` or visually-hidden text if the icon conveys the lock metaphor only.
- The "mark complete" CTA must have a descriptive `aria-label`: `"Mark private coaching complete. This moves the case to the joint session with [other party name]."`.
- The party toggle in solo mode: each button has `aria-pressed` reflecting its active state.

## Color Contrast
All text colors meet WCAG 2.1 AA (≥ 4.5:1 contrast ratio against their background). Specifically:
- `--text-primary` on `--bg-canvas`: verified ≥ 4.5:1 in both themes.
- `--text-secondary` on `--bg-surface`: verified ≥ 4.5:1.
- `--text-tertiary` (timestamps/hints): used only for non-essential decorative text; still targets ≥ 3:1 for large/bold text as per AA.
- `--accent-on` (`#FFFFFF`) on `--accent` (`#6B8E7F`): verify at 4.5:1; if contrast check fails in any variant, switch to `--text-primary` for button label text.
- Never use `--text-tertiary` for anything that conveys meaning that isn't also conveyed by position, shape, or label.

## Screen Reader Copy Patterns (DesignDoc §7.5)
| Element | Announced text |
|---|---|
| Streaming AI message start | `"Coach is replying"` (once) |
| Privacy banner | `"Private conversation. Only you and the AI coach see this."` |
| Mark Complete button | `"Mark private coaching complete. This moves the case to the joint session with [party name]."` |
| Status pill - waiting | `"Waiting on [party name]"` |
| Status pill - your turn | `"Your turn"` |
| Status pill - ready | `"Ready to enter joint session"` |

## Playwright Accessibility Testing
The E2E suite includes a keyboard-only run of the critical path (`solo-full-flow.spec.ts`). Each interactive element must be reachable and activatable with Tab + Enter alone. Use Playwright's `page.keyboard` API for these runs. The `privacy.spec.ts` suite must also assert that `role="log"` containers are present in chat views.
design_rules.motion:
## Duration Tokens
| Token | Value | When |
|---|---|---|
| `--dur-fast` | `150ms` | Hover state changes, button color transitions, modal backdrop |
| `--dur-medium` | `200ms` | Route crossfades, theme switching, panel slides |

No longer durations are defined. If something feels slow at 200ms, the answer is to reduce it, not add a new token.

## Easing
A single easing function is used throughout: `--ease-out: cubic-bezier(0.2, 0.7, 0.3, 1)`. This is a deceleration curve — elements slow as they arrive. Never use `ease-in` for UI transitions (elements speeding up feels aggressive in this emotional context). Never use `linear` for visible transitions.

## Defined Motion Patterns
| Motion | Duration | Easing | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| Route crossfade | `--dur-medium` (200ms) | `--ease-out` | Opacity only. No slide animations. |
| New message fade-in | 150ms | `--ease-out` | Fade-in + 8px upward translate |
| Modal backdrop | `--dur-fast` (150ms) | `--ease-out` | Opacity 0→1 on open |
| Theme switch | `--dur-medium` | `--ease-out` | `background-color` and `color` only |
| Button hover | `--dur-fast` | `--ease-out` | Background and border-color |
| Streaming cursor blink | 1s, `steps(2, start)` | n/a | `@keyframes blink { to { visibility: hidden; } }` |

## Streaming Cursor
The cursor is a thin vertical bar (`width: 2px; height: 1em; vertical-align: text-bottom; background: currentColor; margin-left: 2px`) that blinks at 500ms intervals using `animation: blink 1s steps(2,start) infinite`. It appears only while a message has `status === "STREAMING"` and disappears immediately when `status` transitions to `COMPLETE` or `ERROR`.

## What Motion is NOT Used
- No celebration animation on case closure. The moment is quiet by design (DesignDoc §1.1).
- No "typing" bouncing-dots animation. The streaming cursor replaces it (the bouncing-dot pattern is "too chipper" for the emotional context — DesignDoc §2.6).
- No slide animations on route changes (crossfade only).
- No entrance animations on page load (content renders immediately; skeletons handle the perceived wait).

## prefers-reduced-motion
All three motion categories below must be disabled when `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` matches:
1. Streaming cursor blink animation (`animation: none; visibility: visible;`).
2. Route crossfade (`transition: none`).
3. New message fade-in + translate (set `opacity: 1; transform: none` immediately, no transition).

Implement as a global CSS rule:
```css
@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) {
  *, *::before, *::after {
    animation-duration: 0.01ms !important;
    animation-iteration-count: 1 !important;
    transition-duration: 0.01ms !important;
  }
}
```
Theme-switch transitions may remain since they are triggered by an explicit user action (not ambient).
design_rules.layout:
## Base Grid
8px base grid. All padding, margin, and gap values must be multiples of 8: `8, 16, 24, 32, 48, 64`. Never use arbitrary values like `13px` or `22px` for spacing.

## Content Max-Widths
| Context | Max-width |
|---|---|
| Reading-heavy screens (case form, synthesis view) | `720px` |
| Joint chat | `1080px` |
| Style guide / admin pages | `1200px` |
| Login card | `~400px` |
| Draft Coach panel (desktop) | `420px` |

## Responsive Breakpoint
One primary breakpoint: `768px`. Below it, the layout shifts:
- Draft Coach panel collapses from a right-side `Sheet` to a full-screen bottom sheet.
- Top navigation collapses to a hamburger menu.
- No other major layout shifts are defined for v1 (mobile-responsive web, but not a redesigned mobile experience).

## Page Layout Structure
```
<body>           background: var(--bg-canvas)
  <nav>          top navigation, sticky, backdrop-blur(12px) at 92% canvas opacity
  <main>         flex-column, max-width determined by context, margin: 0 auto
    content      padded 32px horizontal, 64px top, 120px bottom (style guide page)
```

The top navigation (`phase-header`) uses `background: var(--bg-surface); border-bottom: 1px solid var(--border-default); padding: 14px 24px`. It is sticky with a backdrop blur effect on the toolbar variant.

## Chat Layout
- Chat body: `padding: 20px 24px`.
- Message rows: `display: flex; gap: 12px; margin-bottom: 16px`.
- User messages are right-aligned (`flex-direction: row-reverse`).
- Coach/other-party messages are left-aligned.
- Each chat stack max-width is 70% of the chat container width.

## Admin Two-Pane Layout
The template edit view uses a two-column layout: left pane (form fields) and right pane (version history timeline). This is the only two-column content layout in v1. All other content pages are single-column.

## Grid Utilities
The style guide defines `.grid-2`, `.grid-3`, `.grid-4`, `.grid-auto` (auto-fill, minmax 220px). Use these for color swatch / option card grids. For Tailwind implementations, map to `grid-cols-2`, `grid-cols-3`, `grid-cols-4` equivalents.

## Spacing Anti-patterns
- Never use margin/padding values that are not on the 8px grid.
- Never nest multiple `max-width` containers inside each other — pick one and let it govern.
- Never use `vw`/`vh` units for content spacing (only for full-bleed backgrounds or fixed overlays).
design_rules.states-and-feedback:
## Loading States
Use skeleton screens for any load lasting > 300ms. The dashboard shows 3 case-row skeletons on initial load. A case detail page shows the phase-appropriate skeleton layout (chat skeleton for private coaching view, etc.). Never show a spinner for these states.

Exception: synthesis generation uses a text loading state — "Generating your guidance…" — rather than a skeleton, because the synthesis card has a known-in-advance structure that would make the skeleton feel false.

## Streaming / Generating State
AI messages enter the DOM as bubbles with `status="STREAMING"` as soon as the action inserts the row. The bubble renders the accumulating content plus the blinking cursor. The Send button in message inputs is disabled while an AI message is streaming. The copy button does NOT appear on a streaming bubble — only after `status="COMPLETE"`.

"Coach is thinking..." appears inline (below the last message, above the input) when the Coach AI is generating a joint-chat response. This is a reserved visual space even in v1 where presence indicators are deferred.

## Error States
- AI errors render inline as a `bubble-error` (danger-subtle background, danger border) with a [Retry] button. They appear in the message list at the position where the message would have been.
- Network errors (transient) use a Toast notification, not an inline message — they do not represent a lost message, just a connection hiccup.
- Form validation errors render inline below the failing input using `color: var(--danger)`, 13px, `margin-top: 6px`. Never use browser `alert()`.
- Auth errors (magic link delayed, invalid token) render inline below the relevant input or action.

## Empty States
Friendly copy, never cutesy. No illustrations in v1 (text-only empty states). No exclamation marks.
- Dashboard (no cases): "No cases yet. When you're ready to work through something, start a new case."
- Joint chat (no messages — shouldn't happen, but guard): "Coach is preparing…"
- Admin templates (none): "No templates yet. The app will use a built-in default baseline. Create a template when you want to tune the Coach's behavior per category."

## Disabled States
Disabled buttons and inputs use `opacity: 0.5; cursor: not-allowed`. No other visual treatment. The element retains its normal variant styling (primary, secondary, etc.) but is dimmed.

## Confirmation Modals
All destructive or irreversible actions require a `Dialog` modal. The modal describes exactly what will happen: "This will close the case for both of you. Jordan will be notified." Never use `window.confirm()`. The modal must have a clear primary danger action and a cancel ghost action.

## Privacy State
When a screen contains data private to the user, a `PrivacyBanner` is always visible at the top of the content area. It is NOT dismissible. It uses `background: var(--private-tint)` and the Lucide Lock icon. The copy is plain: "Jordan can't see this" — not "This content is restricted from the other party."

## Status Indicator System (Dashboard)
A single-glyph system is used to convey case status at a glance:
- `●` filled circle in `--accent` color: "Your turn" — action required from the logged-in user.
- `○` hollow circle in `--text-secondary`: "Waiting on the other party."
- `◐` half-filled in `--warning`: "Ready to enter joint chat."
- `◼` filled square in `--text-tertiary`: Closed.

These are rendered as `StatusPill` components, not raw characters.
